nodes.label: Account details, customization, and connected domains
nodes.heading: Account details, customization, and connected domains
nodes.snippet:
Account details, customization, and connected domains

Admins can view and manage these settings from your profile picture (top right of the home page) > Account administration > Account settings. From here you can view your account name and domain, customize your logo and theme, brand the mobile app, and connect additional email domains to your account.

Access to Account settings depends on your admin role. Roles with account-level administration access can reach this page.

nodes.label: Account details, customization, and connected domains
nodes.heading: Account details
nodes.snippet:
Account name and domain

Your account name and domain are automatically assigned based on the email domain you signed up with. You can change the domain at any time, as long as no other Kissflow organization is already using it.

To change your domain:

1. Go to your profile picture > Account administration > Account settings > Account details.
2. Click the domain field and enter your new domain.
3. Confirm the change.

Changing your domain logs everyone out of their current session, including you. You're redirected to the new domain shortly after; other users need to navigate to the new domain themselves to sign back in — share the new domain with your team before you switch. Once you move off your old domain, it becomes available for another organization to claim.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Want a fully custom domain instead of .kissflow.com? Custom domain mapping isn't self-service — chat with Kissflow support to set it up.

Account owner

The Account Owner is the person responsible for setting up your Kissflow account and is Kissflow's main point of contact for the account. Only one owner is allowed per account, but the current owner can transfer ownership to another admin at any time.

To transfer ownership:

1. Go to Account settings > Account details.
2. Next to Account Owner, click Change.

Only the current Account Owner sees this option.

3. Search for and select the new owner from the admin list.
4. Confirm the change.

nodes.label: Account details, customization, and connected domains
nodes.heading: Account customization
nodes.snippet:
Account logo

Your account logo is auto-assigned based on the email domain used to sign up, and appears in the top right of your account, on the sign-in screen, and in certain product emails. You can replace it with your own.

1. Go to Account settings > Account customization.
2. Click Replace logo (or Upload a logo if none is set).
3. Choose your image file.

Use a rectangular logo, ideally 240x36 pixels. The file must be under 250 KB and in JPG, PNG, or SVG format.

Mobile app branding

Customize how the Kissflow mobile app (or PWA) looks for your organization.

1. Under Account customization, enter a name in the App name field.
2. Click Replace icon (or Upload an icon if none is set) and choose your image file.

Use an icon that's 512x512 pixels, under 500 KB, and in PNG format.

Account theme

Apply your brand color to header text, links, and backgrounds across your account, and choose an account-wide typeface.

1. Go to your profile picture > Account administration > Account settings > Account customization.
2. Under Theme settings, pick your brand color from the palette or enter its hex code.
- Toggle Apply relevant changes to the background to also carry the color into background elements.
3. Under Select a typeface, choose a font. Once applied, it's used across your entire Kissflow account.
4. Click Preview to check how the color and typeface look before committing.
- Click Save to keep the theme, or Discard changes to go back and adjust it.
- To undo a color or font change, click the reset icon next to it and confirm in the Reset to default value popover.

Preview your theme before saving — lighter shades can affect text readability.

Theme settings and mobile app branding are only available if Kissflow has enabled these features for your account. If you don't see these sections, contact Kissflow support.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

nodes.label: Account details, customization, and connected domains
nodes.heading: Connected domains
nodes.snippet:
If your organization uses multiple email domains, you can connect additional domains to your account. Once a domain is connected, no one else can sign up for a new Kissflow account using it.

For example, if your organization's domain is starkcorporation.co and you also use stark-usa.co, you can connect the second domain to the same account:

1. Click Add a domain.
2. Enter the domain name, domain address, and an email address on that domain to verify it.
3. Submit the request.

An invite email is sent to that address. Once verified, the domain is connected to your account, and no one else can use it to create a new Kissflow account.

You can connect multiple domains to one account, but each domain can only ever belong to a single Kissflow account.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

nodes.label: Account details, customization, and connected domains

- Account security
- Subscription and billing

nodes.label: Admin notification settings
nodes.heading: Admin notification settings
nodes.snippet:
Admin notification settings

Notification settings lets Super Admins control how notifications go out across the entire account — which channels are on by default, which server sends the emails, and what those emails look like.

nodes.label: Admin notification settings
nodes.heading: Global notifications
nodes.snippet:
1. Click your profile picture > Account administration > Notification settings.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

2. Under Global notifications, turn the following toggles on or off for every user on the account:
- Email notifications — sends notifications by email.
- Web app notifications — sends notifications within the web application.
- Push notifications — sends push notifications.
- Bot notifications — sends notifications through the Kissflow bot in Slack, Microsoft Teams, or Google Chat.

Essential system notifications — such as those related to account security and user management — are prioritized and sent regardless of your channel settings, to make sure nobody misses critical updates.

The examples below illustrate the kind of system notifications that bypass channel settings. The exact list may vary and isn't limited to these events.

| Notification | Trigger |
| --- | --- |
| User activated | A user's account is activated. |
| User deactivated | A user's account is deactivated. |
| Resend email verification | The verification email is resent. |
| Email address updated | A user's email address is updated. |
| User created | A new user is created. |
| Reset password | A user's password is reset. |
| 2FA status changed | A user's two-factor authentication status is changed. |
| Two factor verification | Two-factor authentication is enabled with an email code. |
| Domain verification | A verification email is sent to connect a domain. |
| Waiting for admin approval | A user creates a process, board, or dataset that requires admin approval. |
| Export | An export related to a user, board, dataform, dataset, or report is carried out. |

media.alt: Account administration menu with Notification settings highlighted

nodes.label: Admin notification settings
nodes.heading: SMTP server settings
nodes.snippet:
SMTP server settings are available on Enterprise plans only.

By default, Kissflow sends notification emails from its own server. Super Admins can configure a custom SMTP server instead — useful if you want to route emails through your own infrastructure, or if you need to skip email verification for new users.

Click your profile picture > Account administration > Notification settings > SMTP server settings.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Add a custom SMTP server

1. Click Add custom SMTP server.
2. Fill in the server details:
- SMTP server name — a name to identify this configuration.
- Host — your SMTP server's host address.
- Port — the port number.
- Security protocol — STARTTLS or SSL/TLS.
- Authentication type:
- Basic auth — enter the Username and Password for your SMTP server.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}
- OAuth 2.0 — choose Google or Microsoft from the provider dropdown. You'll be prompted to sign in and authorize Kissflow to access the email account.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}
3. Configure the Sender profile — Sender name, Sender email, and Reply-to email.

By default, this profile applies to every environment (Development, Test, and Production).

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

To override it for a specific environment:

1. Click the environments link in the banner to see your list of environments.
2. Click Manage on the environment you want to customize — for example, Development.
3. Go to Notification settings > SMTP server settings.
4. Click Create profile — visible once your server is successfully configured — enter the sender details for that environment, and save.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}
4. Under Server failure alerts, enter a recipient email address to be notified if the server fails to send emails.
5. Click Save and test. Your configuration is saved immediately, and Kissflow runs a connection test in the background — if the test fails, the server card shows a failed validation status, but your saved configuration isn't affected.
6. To switch between the Default server and your custom server at any time, click the More options icon on either card and select Switch to this server.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Skip email verification

The Skip email verification option lets new users receive notification emails immediately, without first verifying their email address.

This is only available with a custom SMTP server — you can't enable it while using the default Kissflow server.

Toggle Skip email verification ON on your custom SMTP server card.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

If you haven't configured a custom server yet, switching this toggle prompts you to add one.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Edit an active custom SMTP server

You can't change a custom SMTP server's configuration while it's actively sending emails.

1. Click the Edit icon on the active server card.
2. In the confirmation popup, click Disable and edit.
3. Update the server details or sender profile.
4. Click Save and test.
5. Once you're ready to use it again, switch back to the custom SMTP server.

While you're editing, the account temporarily switches to the Default server so email delivery isn't interrupted.

Delete an active custom SMTP server

1. Click the Delete icon on the active server card.
2. In the confirmation popup, click Disable and delete.

The account automatically reverts to the Default server once the custom server is deleted.

Edit an inactive custom SMTP server

If a custom server isn't currently active, you can update it directly — no disable step required.

1. Click the Edit icon on the inactive server card.
2. Update the server details, credentials, or sender profile.
3. Click Save and test.

Delete an inactive custom SMTP server

1. Click the Delete icon on the inactive server card.
2. Click Delete to confirm.

media.alt: SMTP server settings page showing the Default server and custom server cards
Basic auth fields for username and password
OAuth 2.0 provider dropdown with Google and Microsoft options
Sender profile fields with the environments banner
Creating an environment-specific sender profile
More options icon
Switching between the default and custom SMTP server
Skip email verification toggle on the custom SMTP server card
Prompt to add a custom SMTP server when toggling skip verification
Edit icon
Delete icon

nodes.label: Admin notification settings
nodes.heading: Customizing account notifications
nodes.snippet:
Under Customize notifications, Super Admins can edit the content of emails sent from the account — the welcome email, the resend-verification email, and the header and footer that wrap every email.

Editing emails

1. Under the General tab, click Edit on the welcome email or the resend-verification email.
2. Use the built-in editor to add, remove, or reformat text. Insert custom fields and system fields — like name, email, or phone number — to pull in user-specific information.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}
3. Click Preview to check your changes, then Save.

Signature fields aren't supported in these email templates, default or customized.

Click Reset at any time to restore the original default content.

Header and footer

Under the Header and footer tab, edit or view the header and footer that appear on every email sent from your account — useful for consistent branding, contact information, or legal disclaimers.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Email editor with custom and system fields
Email header configuration
Email footer configuration

nodes.label: Bot settings
nodes.heading: Bot settings
nodes.snippet:
Bot settings

As a Super Admin, you can enable the Kissflow Bot for your organization's chat app — Slack, Microsoft Teams, or Google Chat. Once enabled, members can sign in to Kissflow from the chat app and receive real-time notifications about their processes and boards without switching tabs, then act on them (view, approve, reject, add notes) right from the chat.

media.alt: Kissflow x Chat apps - Overview video

nodes.label: Bot settings
nodes.heading: Enable the Kissflow Bot
nodes.snippet:
1. Click your profile picture in the top right corner and go to Account administration > Account settings > Bot settings.
2. Turn on the toggle for each chat app your organization uses: Google Chat, Slack, or Microsoft Teams.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Once a chat app is turned on, members can sign in to it using the steps below.

media.alt: Bot settings page with toggles for Google Chat, Slack, and Microsoft Teams

nodes.label: Bot settings
nodes.heading: Sign in to Kissflow from Google Chat
nodes.snippet:
1. Install the Kissflow app:
1. Go to the Google Workspace Marketplace.
2. Find the Kissflow app and install it.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

2. Look for Kissflow in your Google Chat list.
3. Sign in to your Kissflow account:
1. Type /login inside the Kissflow app and press Enter to sign in. A login URL is generated automatically.
2. Click the login URL, enter your Kissflow account domain, and click Submit.
3. Click Allow in the permissions popup that appears.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Once signed in, you'll start receiving notifications from your processes and boards directly in the Kissflow app within Google Chat. To sign out, type /logout and press Enter.

media.alt: Kissflow app listing in the Google Workspace Marketplace
Permissions popup for signing in to Kissflow from Google Chat

nodes.label: Bot settings
nodes.heading: Sign in to Kissflow from Microsoft Teams
nodes.snippet:
1. Install the Kissflow app:
1. On Microsoft Teams, click Apps in the left sidebar.
2. Find the Kissflow app and add it.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

2. Open your newly added Kissflow chat.
3. Sign in to your Kissflow account:
1. Type login (no slash) inside the Kissflow app and press Enter to sign in. A login URL is generated automatically.
2. Click the login URL, enter your Kissflow account domain, and click Submit.
3. Click Allow in the permissions popup that appears.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Once signed in, you'll start receiving notifications from your processes and boards directly in the Kissflow app chat within Microsoft Teams. To sign out, type logout and press Enter.

media.alt: Kissflow app in the Microsoft Teams Apps list
Permissions popup for signing in to Kissflow from Microsoft Teams
Kissflow App for Microsoft Teams

nodes.label: Bot settings
nodes.heading: Sign in to Kissflow from Slack
nodes.snippet:
1. Install the Kissflow app:
1. On Slack, click Apps.
2. Find the Kissflow app and add it.
2. Open your newly added Kissflow chat.
3. Sign in to your Kissflow account:
1. Type /login inside the Kissflow app and press Enter to sign in. A login URL is generated automatically.
2. Click the login URL, enter your Kissflow account domain, and click Submit.
3. Click Allow in the permissions popup that appears.

Once signed in, you'll start receiving notifications from your processes and boards directly in the Kissflow app chat within Slack. To sign out, type /logout and press Enter.

media.alt: Kissflow Apps for Slack

nodes.label: Bot settings
nodes.heading: Notification actions
nodes.snippet:
Members can act on a notification directly from the card that appears in their chat app:

| Module | Available actions |
| ------- | ------------------------------------- |
| Process | View item, Approve item, Reject item |
| Board | View item, Add note |

nodes.label: Bot settings
nodes.heading: Notification events
nodes.snippet:
Notifications from processes and boards are sent to the chat app for the following events:

| Module | Event | Recipient |
| ------- | ------------------------- | --------------------- |
| Process | Item assigned to a user | Assignee |
| Process | Item approved | Item initiator |
| Process | Item rejected | Item initiator |
| Process | Item reassigned | Newly assigned user |
| Process | Item deadline breached | Assignee |
| Board | Item assigned to a user | Assignee |
| Board | Item deadline breached | Assignee |
| Board | User mentioned in a note | Mentioned user |

The events and recipients above reflect standard notification behavior. Your organization's notification settings can further customize which events are sent for specific processes, boards, or apps.

nodes.label: File upload settings
nodes.heading: File upload settings
nodes.snippet:
File upload settings

As a Super Admin, or an IAM Admin, you control which file attachment services account users can upload files from in Kissflow.

nodes.label: File upload settings
nodes.heading: Choose your attachment services
nodes.snippet:
1. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner of the Home screen.
2. Click Account administration > Account settings > File upload settings.
3. Turn the toggle on or off for each service you want to allow:
- Google Drive
- Dropbox
- Box
- OneDrive
- Local files

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

At least one service must stay enabled. Kissflow won't let you turn off the last remaining toggle, since users need at least one way to upload attachments.

media.alt: File upload settings page with toggles for Google Drive, Dropbox, Box, OneDrive, and Local files

nodes.label: File upload settings
nodes.heading: How Kissflow secures your attachments
nodes.snippet:
All Kissflow attachments are hosted in Google Cloud Storage (GCS). Data is encrypted in transit and at rest using GCS's standard security policies, with Kissflow's application and GCS mutually authenticating each other.

To upload or download an attachment, Kissflow generates a secure link so the file transfer happens directly between the client and GCS instead of routing through additional hops.

Kissflow also blocks manipulated or disguised files from being uploaded. For example, if only JPGs are allowed for a field and someone tries to upload a .exe file renamed with a .jpg extension, Kissflow detects the mismatch and blocks it. This check is based on the file's metadata — its name, extension, and declared type — rather than inspecting the file's contents, for privacy.

If you can't preview an uploaded file, it's likely disguised or invalid. Treat this as a safety flag worth double-checking.

nodes.label: File upload settings
nodes.heading: Validation rules for attachment fields
nodes.snippet:
Beyond account-wide upload settings, admins building a flow's form can add validation rules to individual attachment fields — including Smart attachment fields and attachments on multilingual and public forms. These rules are set on the Validations tab of the field:

- Minimum number of attachments — the least number of files required for the field.
- Maximum number of attachments — the most files a user can add to the field.
- File type restrictions — limit uploads to specific formats, including images (JPG, JPEG, PNG, BMP, GIF, TIF), documents (DOC, DOCX, PDF), presentations (PPT, PPTX), spreadsheets (XLS, XLSX), and others (CSV, TXT, EML).
- Attachment size limit — cap file size, up to 100 MB.

For more on configuring these fields, see Image, Attachment, Smart attachment, and Signature fields.

nodes.label: File upload settings
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:
- User roles and permissions
- Image, Attachment, Smart attachment, and Signature fields

nodes.label: Flow settings
nodes.heading: Flow settings
nodes.snippet:
Flow settings

Super Admins and IAM Admins can control who's allowed to create new flows in your account, enable or disable flow types, and set account-wide print, download, and email action permissions.

nodes.label: Flow settings
nodes.heading: Access flow settings
nodes.snippet:
1. Click your profile picture at the top right.
2. Select Account administration > Flow settings.

All flow types — process, board, dataset, and integration — are enabled by default.

nodes.label: Flow settings
nodes.heading: Enable or disable a flow type
nodes.snippet:
1. On the Flow settings page, click the flow type you want to configure.
2. Toggle the flow type on or off.

If you disable a flow type, its existing data becomes temporarily inaccessible and no one can create a new flow of that type until you enable it again.

nodes.label: Flow settings
nodes.heading: Control who can create a new flow
nodes.snippet:
1. On the Flow settings page, click the flow type you want to configure.
2. Under Who can create a new flow?, choose one of three options:
- Anyone — every user in the organization can create a new flow of this type without approval.
- Super Admins — only the Account Owner and Super Admins can create a new flow.
- Anyone, after the approval of — any user can request a new flow, but it's created only after the approvers you specify approve the request. Use the dropdown to add or remove approvers.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Changing this setting only affects new flow requests going forward — it doesn't change access on flows that already exist.

media.alt: Flow settings page showing the who-can-create options for a flow type

nodes.label: Flow settings
nodes.heading: Print and download permissions
nodes.snippet:
For process, board, and dataset flows, you can control whether users can print forms or download attachments.

1. On the Flow settings page, click the flow type you want to configure.
2. Under Print and Download, toggle between:
- Anyone — any user can print or download.
- Flow Admins — only Flow Admins can print or download.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

This is an Account Administration–level setting. When it's set to Flow Admins, it takes precedence over any print or download permission configured at the individual flow's security settings.

media.alt: Flow settings page showing the print and download toggle for a flow type

nodes.label: Flow settings
nodes.heading: Email actions
nodes.snippet:
For process flows, Email actions lets assignees approve or reject items directly from their email inbox, without logging into Kissflow.

1. On the Flow settings page, click Process.
2. Toggle Email actions on.

Learn more in Email actions.

- User roles and permissions
- Email actions

nodes.label: Format settings
nodes.heading: Format settings
nodes.snippet:
Format settings

As a Super Admin — or any admin granted the Format Settings permission — you control your account's default time zone, language, and date format. These settings determine how dates, times, and flow data are displayed across your organization.

Individual users can override the account's time zone, date format, and language for their own account from My settings. See Personal info, format settings, and task delegation.

nodes.label: Format settings
nodes.heading: Update format settings
nodes.snippet:
1. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner of the Home screen.
2. Click Account administration > Format settings.
3. Set your account's time zone, language, and date format (details below).
4. Click Save.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Time zone

Your organization's time zone is used when a scheduled action in an integration runs, or when a deadline in a workflow is evaluated — all flow data is matched to this time zone.

Select a time zone directly from the dropdown, or use the map below it: click a region to select its time zone, and use the zoom controls to find smaller zones more precisely.

Language

Choose the display language for your account. Kissflow supports more than 20 languages, including English, French, Spanish, German, Italian, Russian, Korean, Simplified Chinese, Japanese, Vietnamese, Thai, Polish, Portuguese, Slovak, Czech, Ukrainian, Hindi, and Romanian.

Arabic is the exception — it's only available if your account has been enabled for it (see Languages supported by Kissflow).

Date format

Choose how date and datetime fields are displayed across your account:

- Local — based on your browser settings.
- US — M/D/YYYY.
- European — D/M/YYYY.
- ISO — YYYY-MM-DD.

Date format only affects date and datetime fields. Number and currency formatting is separate — see below.

Number and currency format

Number and currency formatting is always based on the browser's local conventions and isn't configurable from this page.

media.alt: Format settings page with the timezone map, language, and date format options

nodes.label: Geolocation settings
nodes.heading: Access geolocation settings
nodes.snippet:
1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner of the page.
3. Click Account administration > Account settings > Geolocation settings.

nodes.label: Geolocation settings
nodes.heading: Add or update the Google Maps API key
nodes.snippet:
1. On the Geolocation settings page, enter your Google Maps API key in the input box.

You can use an existing Google Maps API key or create a new one. Click the Google Maps API key link on the page for instructions on Google's developer documentation site.

2. Save your changes.

After you add or update the API key, notify your users to reload Kissflow so the change takes effect.

nodes.label: Geolocation settings
nodes.heading: When the API key is missing or invalid
nodes.snippet:
If Kissflow can't load Google Maps for the Geolocation field, the message shown depends on where the form is being filled out:

- On the Kissflow mobile app, users see a message asking them to contact their Super Admin to add the Google Maps API key.
- On web, users see a general connection error instead, without a direct mention of the API key.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

In either case, an Account Admin needs to add or fix the key in Geolocation settings to resolve it.

media.alt: Geolocation field on a form showing a Google Maps loading error

nodes.label: Subscription and billing
nodes.heading: Subscription and billing
nodes.snippet:
Subscription and billing

Your account's subscription details — plan status, user license count, and invoices — are all available from Account administration.

Only Super Admins and Billing Admins can access this page. Learn more about roles in Kissflow.

nodes.label: Subscription and billing
nodes.heading: View your subscription details
nodes.snippet:
1. Click your profile picture in the top-right corner and select Account administration.
2. Go to Subscription & billing.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

This page shows the current status of your plan and the number of user licenses available under it.

Kissflow offers several plan tiers beyond the basics. To purchase additional licenses, change plans, or adjust your billing frequency, click Chat for help under Subscription details — see Deprecation of self-serve option in Kissflow subscription & billing.

media.alt: Subscription and billing page showing plan status and user license details

nodes.label: Subscription and billing
nodes.heading: View and download an invoice
nodes.snippet:
1. Go to Account administration > Subscription & billing.
2. Click View invoice.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

From here, you can view and download the invoice for your account.

media.alt: Subscription and billing page with the View invoice action

nodes.label: Task delegation
nodes.heading: Task delegation
nodes.snippet:
Task delegation

From Account Administration, Super Admins can delegate a user's future process tasks to another user — for example, when someone is out of office. You can delegate all the processes a user is part of, or only specific ones.

Only new process tasks are delegated. Tasks already assigned to the user when you set up the delegation stay with them.

nodes.label: Task delegation
nodes.heading: Task delegation table
nodes.snippet:
The task delegation table lists every delegation in the account, split across two tabs:

- Set by admins — delegations Super Admins have created.
- Set by users — delegations users have set up for themselves.

From this table you can create a new delegation, and view, edit, or cancel any existing one.

nodes.label: Task delegation
nodes.heading: Add a new delegation
nodes.snippet:
1. Click your profile picture > Account administration > Task delegation > + New.
2. Select the user whose tasks you want to delegate.
3. Select the start and end dates for the delegation.
4. Choose whether to delegate tasks from all processes the user is part of, or only specific processes.
5. Select who the tasks should go to:
- If you chose All processes, select the single user to delegate to.
- If you chose Specific processes, you can select a different user for each process.
6. To delegate several processes to the same user in one go, select their checkboxes, click Delegate to, and choose the user.
7. Click Create.

Notifications for delegated process steps are redirected to the delegated user. The Workflow completed notification is the exception — it always goes to the item's original requester or initiator, even if they've delegated their tasks.

You can view and edit an existing delegation any time by selecting it from the Task delegation table and clicking Edit delegation.

When the user returns, they're greeted with a welcome-back message about the delegation. They can review and cancel it from there to resume acting on their items, or cancel it later from My settings > Task delegation.

nodes.label: Task delegation
nodes.heading: Override a delegation set by a user
nodes.snippet:
As a Super Admin, you can override a delegation a user has set up for themselves.

1. Under the Set by users tab, hover over the delegation you want to override.
2. Click Override.
3. Make the changes you need, then click Override again to confirm.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Once you've overridden a user's delegation, that row can't be overridden again — it shows as override-restricted. You can still edit your existing override from the same table.

A Super Admin's override always takes priority over the user's own delegation. Once the override ends, the user's original delegation automatically resumes for whatever duration is left.

Example: A user sets a 15-day delegation. You override it with a 5-day delegation. Your delegation applies first — once those 5 days pass, the user's delegation picks back up automatically for the remaining 10 days.

media.alt: Task delegation table with the Override action on a user-set delegation

nodes.label: Task delegation
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:

nodes.label: Weekends and holidays
nodes.heading: Weekends and holidays
nodes.snippet:
Weekends and holidays

As a Super Admin, you can set your organization's workweek and holidays so they're factored in when deadlines and SLAs are calculated for workflow activities.

nodes.label: Weekends and holidays
nodes.heading: Set your organization's weekend
nodes.snippet:
1. Click your profile picture in the top right corner and go to Account administration > Account settings > Weekends and holidays.
2. Under Weekends, click each day your organization doesn't work to select it.

No weekend days are selected by default. Set your weekend here before it's needed for deadline or SLA calculations.

nodes.label: Weekends and holidays
nodes.heading: Add office holidays
nodes.snippet:
1. Click Add holiday.
2. Select the date.
3. Give the holiday a name.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Holidays don't recur automatically. Your holiday list is for a single year, so you'll need to add next year's holidays yourself when the time comes.

media.alt: Weekends and holidays settings page showing the weekly working days and holiday list

nodes.label: Weekends and holidays
nodes.heading: How this affects deadlines and SLAs
nodes.snippet: Weekend days and holidays you've configured here are excluded from deadline and SLA calculations for workflow activities. If part of a task's allotted time falls on a non-working day, that day isn't counted — the deadline extends to give the assignee the full amount of working time you intended.

nodes.label: Audit log
nodes.heading: Audit log
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

nodes.label: Audit log
nodes.heading: Overview
nodes.snippet:
The audit log is a centralized record of all activities that take place in your Kissflow account. It helps administrators track who did what, when, and from where by capturing events across your account, such as, user activity, security-related events or changes made to account settings and flows.

Audit logs preserve the record of actions taken in your account, giving admins clarity, traceability, and oversight. You can also filter for specific events and export logs for compliance or analysis purposes.

The account-level audit log is available to the Super Admins, IAM Admins (account management logs) and User Admins (user and group management logs). To view audit log, go to Profile > Account administration > Audit log.

Similarly, audit logs for flows is available to flow admins across process, boards, datasets, integrations, apps and portals. They can be accessed by clicking the More options button > Audit log.

You can get the following details from the Audit log section of your account:

- Timestamp: The exact time when a specific action happened or was performed.
- Acted by: The account user who acted.
- Event: The kind of activity or action that had taken place. Examples include Dataset permission change, Account ownership transferred, User activated, Account security updated, Field modified, IP address, etc.
- Event category: Common name for an event that you can use to filter down to similar events. Examples: Process Administration, Case Administration, etc.,
- Object: The resource that is updated.
- Audit ID: This unique ID is generated automatically by the system for every event recorded as part of the history.
- Platform: The device or Operating System (OS) used to record the event.
- IP: The network IP address of the recording device.
- Batch ID: This unique ID is generated automatically by the system for a group of related events. Unlike Audit ID, the Batch ID of these events will be the same for reference purposes.

Activities across the account appear in the audit log with a delay, typically from a few minutes to a few hours in some cases.

nodes.label: Audit log
nodes.heading: Interpreting the audit log
nodes.snippet:
When you click on an entry in the audit log, the Event log panel opens on the right as shown in the gif below. You will see an AI-generated summary followed by the complete metadata and you can use the navigation arrows to move to the previous or next record without closing the panel. More about each section here:

- AI summary: This is a short AI-generated summary at the top of the panel providing a quick overview of the event before going through the raw details.

- Complete metadata view: Below the AI summary, the event metadata is displayed in a structured format. The keys and values are displayed in JSON format, and you can expand or collapse nested objects to drill down into deeper details.

- Navigation controls: You can move between events directly from this panel using the arrow controls at the top as shown in the gif below.

Using filters

When you click Show filters, additional filtering options appear, letting you narrow down the audit log results. You can hide the filter panel anytime by selecting Hide filters, to keep the focus back on the log entries.

You will be able to filter the Audit log events based on the following fields/criteria:

- Audit period
1. Fixed date range: Get event logs for the Last 7 days, Last 30 days or Last 90 days.
2. Custom date range: Select a customized date range (within the last 30 days) to filter events.
- Object: Select one or more objects from the suggestions or enter the exact object name you would like to derive the log details for.
- Event category: Filtering events based on Event category is a recommended way to get specific events from the Audit log. If you are unsure how to go with the event category, enter a flow name like _process_ or _board_ in its search bar, and it will list all the categories relevant to the entry.

- Acted by: You can filter events based on users.
- Event: Select one from the suggested list of actions as necessary. You can filter events based on one action at a time.
- Field name: This filter lets you drill deeper into specific attributes of an event. You can combine it with other filters for more precise results.

For example, in the gif below, the Acted by filter is set to _Neon S_, and the Field name filter is set to _LastLoggedIn_. This helps you audit all records related to Neon’s login activity. You can move through the entries using the previous/next arrows in the event panel. You can investigate each login and logout event in detail for _Neon S_ and get a clear picture of his access timeline.

- Audit ID, Batch ID, and IP: Filters are applied on these fields mostly to backtrace an event's history. Enter the exact Audit ID, Batch ID, or IP address to filter events as necessary.
- Platform : Narrow down audit log results based on the operating system of the device from which the action was performed.
- To clear a specific filter condition, click the Remove button .

nodes.label: Audit log
nodes.heading: Sorting and pinning columns
nodes.snippet: Alternatively you could sort records in ascending or descending order to get faster to the desired event log. You can also pin a column, keeping it fixed while scrolling horizontally, so important details stay visible at all times.

nodes.label: Audit log
nodes.heading: Exporting an audit log
nodes.snippet:
Click Export  to download your audit logs. In the export dialog, you can choose how you want the data delivered:

- Filtered data – Export only the results currently shown after applying filters.
- All data from the last 90 days – Export the complete activity log for the past 90 days.

Optionally, convert all date and time fields in the log to match the time zone set in your My settings. Once you confirm, a link to the CSV file (packaged in a ZIP format) will be sent to your registered email address.

> Note:
> For export needs beyond a 90-day period, please reach out to customer support.

nodes.label: Event categories and actions
nodes.heading: Event categories and actions
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Event category is a group of actions, to help admins monitor, review, and audit operations efficiently from the audit log page. These categories include process administration, board administration and the like.

An action refers to the specific activity carried out by users in an account like dataset permission changes, user activation, or field modifications. Each set of action is mapped to an event category, making it easier to track for events.

This documentation lists all event categories and their corresponding actions.

> Note:
>
> Other actions not listed in this documentation fall under the unclassified event category.

nodes.label: Backing up data to Amazon S3
nodes.heading: Backing up data to Amazon S3
nodes.snippet:
Backing up data to Amazon S3

Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3) is one of the storage destinations you can configure for Kissflow's data backup feature, alongside Google Drive, Google Cloud Storage, and email delivery. Once configured, Kissflow exports your account data as JSON files, packages them into ZIP files, and uploads them directly to the S3 bucket you specify.

Data backup is available on request. Contact Kissflow support to enable it for your account.

To configure backup preferences, you need admin access to the Data backup section of your account — see User roles and permissions for how backup access is granted.

nodes.label: Backing up data to Amazon S3
nodes.heading: Before you start
nodes.snippet:
Grant the following Amazon S3 bucket permissions in your IAM policy:

- s3:PutObject — allows Kissflow to add backup files to your bucket.
- s3:GetObject — allows Kissflow to fetch objects from your bucket.
- s3:ListBucket — allows Kissflow to list the objects in your bucket.

You'll also need an AWS access key ID and secret access key for authentication. Copy these to your clipboard, or download them as a CSV file — either works when you authenticate inside Kissflow.

{/* TODO: screenshot — source image expired (Google CDN); recapture from product UI */}

nodes.label: Backing up data to Amazon S3
nodes.heading: Start a backup configuration
nodes.snippet:
1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click Account administration > Data backup.
3. Click Start configuration on the Data backup page.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Setting up a backup involves three steps: authenticating your storage destination, managing backup preferences, and selecting the data categories to include.

media.alt: Data backup page with the Start configuration button

nodes.label: Backing up data to Amazon S3
nodes.heading: Authenticate your Amazon S3 account
nodes.snippet:
1. Select Amazon S3 as the storage mode from the dropdown.
2. Click Upload CSV to auto-populate your AWS access key ID and secret access key.

You can alternatively paste your copied credentials directly into the Access Key ID and Secret Access Key fields instead of uploading a CSV.

3. Click Next.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

To change your AWS credentials later, click Replace CSV and upload a new CSV file with a different access key ID and secret access key.

media.alt: Storage mode authentication step with Amazon S3 selected and AWS credential fields

nodes.label: Backing up data to Amazon S3
nodes.heading: Manage backup preferences
nodes.snippet:
1. Set the file location. Paste the S3 bucket URI where you want your backup files stored, in the form s3://your-bucket-name. You can copy this from the bucket's details page in the AWS console.
2. Choose a ZIP file size. Select 4 GB, 8 GB, 16 GB, or 24 GB. Kissflow automatically zips and splits your backup according to the limit you choose — for example, a 24 GB limit applied to a 100 GB backup produces four 24 GB files and one 4 GB file. Files 24 GB or smaller upload unchanged.
3. Encrypt the backup file (optional). Turn on the switch to add a passphrase that encrypts your backup for additional security. Your passphrase must:
- Contain at least eight characters.
- Contain both upper and lower case letters.
- Contain at least one numeric character or symbol.

Save your passphrase securely. You'll need it to access your backup file later, and Kissflow can't recover it for you.

4. Click Next.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Backup preferences step showing file location, ZIP size, and encryption options

nodes.label: Backing up data to Amazon S3
nodes.heading: Select data categories to back up
nodes.snippet:
Choose one or more categories to include in your backup:

- User and group data — all account user and group data.
- Transactional data — flow item details such as status, comments, and members involved.
- Audit log data — the last year of audit logs at both the flow and account levels.
- Attachments — attachments in flows such as processes, boards, and more.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Data category selection step with checkboxes for user and group data, transactional data, audit logs, and attachments

nodes.label: Backing up data to Amazon S3
nodes.heading: Review and start the backup

media.alt: Backup confirmation popup summarizing the configuration before starting the backup
Data backup page showing options to edit, delete, or view backup history for a saved configuration

nodes.label: Backing up data to Amazon S3
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:
- Data backup overview
- Understanding your backup output
- Compliance and backup history
- Backing up data to Google Drive
- Backing up data to Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
- Backing up data to Kissflow

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
nodes.heading: Backing up data to Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
nodes.snippet:
Backing up data to Google Cloud Storage (GCS)

Data backup is available on request. Contact Kissflow support to enable it on your account.

Google Cloud Storage is one of the storage destinations you can configure for account data backups, alongside Amazon S3, Google Drive, and Kissflow's own storage. Once configured, Kissflow exports your account data as JSON files, packages them into ZIP files, and uploads them directly to the GCS bucket you specify.

As a Super Admin, you can configure Kissflow to back up your account data to a Google Cloud Storage (GCS) bucket you control.

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
nodes.heading: Prerequisites
nodes.snippet:
Before you start configuration, grant these permissions in your Google Cloud IAM policy for the target bucket:

- storage.buckets.get — returns metadata for the bucket.
- storage.objects.create — allows Kissflow to write backup files to the bucket.
- storage.objects.get — allows Kissflow to read object metadata from the bucket.

You'll also need a service account key downloaded as a JSON file — you upload this file inside Kissflow to authenticate.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Animation of creating a Google Cloud service account key

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
nodes.heading: Start data backup configuration
nodes.snippet:
1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click Account administration > Data backup.
3. Click Start configuration on the Data backup page.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Configuration involves four steps: authenticating a storage mode, managing backup settings, selecting data categories, and reviewing your configuration before you start the backup.

media.alt: Data backup page with the Start configuration button

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
nodes.heading: Authenticate your GCS account
nodes.snippet:
1. Select Google Cloud Storage as the storage mode from the dropdown.
2. Click Upload JSON and select the service account key file you downloaded from Google Cloud. This auto-populates your GCS service account keys.
3. Click Next.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

To change your service account keys later, click Replace JSON and upload a new key file.

media.alt: Storage mode dropdown with Google Cloud Storage selected and the Upload JSON button

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
nodes.heading: Manage backup settings
nodes.snippet:
1. Bucket URI. Paste the GCS bucket URI where your backup files should be stored, in the form gs://your-bucket-name. You can copy this from the bucket's details page in Google Cloud Console.
2. Select ZIP file size. Choose 4 GB, 8 GB, 16 GB, or 24 GB. Files in your account are automatically zipped and split according to your chosen limit.

For example, a 24 GB limit applied to a 100 GB export produces four 24 GB files and one 4 GB file. Files 24 GB or smaller upload unchanged.

3. Encrypt backup file (optional). Switch on this toggle to add a passphrase that encrypts your backed-up files for additional security. Your passphrase must contain:
- At least eight characters.
- Both upper and lower case characters.
- At least one numeric character or symbol.

Save your passphrase somewhere secure. You'll need it to decrypt and access your backed-up files later, and it can't be recovered if lost.

4. Click Save to continue to the next step, where you select your data categories.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Backup preferences with bucket URI, ZIP file size, and encryption options

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
nodes.heading: Select data categories for backup
nodes.snippet:
Choose one or more categories to include:

- User and group data — all account user and group data.
- Transactional data — flow item details such as status, comments, and assignees.
- Audit log data — the last year's audit logs at both the flow and account levels.
- Attachments — attachments across flows, including processes, boards, datasets, and apps.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Select your categories and click Next. In the confirmation popup, verify your configuration details and click Start backup.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Not ready to start? Save your configuration and exit — you can resume it later instead of running the backup immediately.

From the Data backup home page, you can also edit your configuration, delete it, or view your backup history.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Data category selection with checkboxes for user and group data, transactional data, audit logs, and attachments
Backup confirmation popup showing configuration details and the Start backup button
Data backup home page with options to edit configuration, delete it, or view backup history

- Backing up data to Kissflow

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Drive
nodes.heading: Backing up data to Google Drive
nodes.snippet:
Backing up data to Google Drive

Google Drive is one of the storage destinations you can configure for Kissflow's data backup feature, alongside Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, and email delivery. Once configured, Kissflow exports your account data as JSON files, packages them into ZIP files, and uploads them directly to the Google Drive folder you specify.

Data backup is available on request. Contact Kissflow support to enable it for your account.

To configure backup preferences, you need admin access to the Data backup section of your account — see User roles and permissions for how backup access is granted.

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Drive
nodes.heading: Start a backup configuration
nodes.snippet:
1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click Account administration > Data backup.
3. Click Start configuration on the Data backup page.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Setting up a backup involves four steps: authenticating your storage destination, managing backup preferences, selecting the data categories to include, and reviewing your configuration before you start the backup.

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Drive
nodes.heading: Authenticate your Google Drive account
nodes.snippet:
1. Select Google Drive as the storage mode from the dropdown.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

2. Sign in with your Google account.
3. Click Next.

media.alt: Storage mode authentication step with Google Drive selected

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Drive
nodes.heading: Manage backup preferences
nodes.snippet:
1. Set the file location. Paste or browse to the Google Drive folder where you want your backup files stored.
2. Choose a ZIP file size. Select 4 GB, 8 GB, 16 GB, or 24 GB. Kissflow automatically zips and splits your backup according to the limit you choose — for example, a 24 GB limit applied to a 100 GB backup produces four 24 GB files and one 4 GB file. Files 24 GB or smaller upload unchanged.
3. Encrypt the backup file (optional). Turn on the switch to add a passphrase that encrypts your backup for additional security. Your passphrase must:
- Contain at least eight characters.
- Contain both upper and lower case letters.
- Contain at least one numeric character or symbol.

Save your passphrase securely. You'll need it to access your backup file later, and Kissflow can't recover it for you.

4. Click Next.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Backup preferences step showing file location, ZIP size, and encryption options

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Drive
nodes.heading: Select data categories to back up
nodes.snippet:
Choose one or more categories to include in your backup:

- User and group data — all account user and group data.
- Transactional data — flow item details such as status, comments, and members involved.
- Audit log data — the last year of audit logs at both the flow and account levels.
- Attachments — attachments in flows such as processes, boards, and more.

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Drive
nodes.heading: Review and start the backup
nodes.snippet:
1. Select your data categories and click Next. A backup confirmation popup opens, summarizing your storage mode, file location, uploaded file size, and selected data categories.
2. Verify the details and click Start backup.

You can only back up your data once every six months, and a backup can't be canceled once it starts. Review your configuration carefully before clicking Start backup.

If you save your configuration and exit without starting the backup, you can resume it later from the same page. From there, you can also edit or delete a configuration, or view your backup history.

nodes.label: Backing up data to Kissflow
nodes.heading: Backing up data to Kissflow
nodes.snippet:
Backing up data to Kissflow

Data backup is available on request. Contact Kissflow support to enable it on your account.

This is the simplest of Kissflow's backup destinations: instead of authenticating a cloud storage account, you choose Email as the storage mode and receive a downloadable link once the backup completes. As a Super Admin, you configure preferences once and reuse them for every future backup.

You can only back up your data once every six months, and a backup can't be canceled once it starts. Plan your configuration and timing accordingly.

nodes.label: Backing up data to Kissflow
nodes.heading: Start data backup configuration
nodes.snippet:
1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click Account administration > Data backup.
3. Click Start configuration on the Data backup page.

Configuration involves three steps: authenticating a storage mode, managing backup settings, and selecting data categories.

nodes.label: Backing up data to Kissflow
nodes.heading: Select email as your storage mode
nodes.snippet:
1. Select Email as the storage mode from the dropdown.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

2. Click Next.

After the backup completes, you'll receive a downloadable link by email. Download the file within seven days of receiving that email — the link expires after that.

media.alt: Data backup storage mode dropdown with Email selected

nodes.label: Backing up data to Kissflow
nodes.heading: Manage backup settings
nodes.snippet:
1. Select ZIP file size. Choose 4 GB, 8 GB, 16 GB, or 24 GB. Files in your account are automatically zipped and split according to your chosen limit.

For example, a 24 GB limit applied to a 100 GB export produces four 24 GB files and one 4 GB file. Files 24 GB or smaller upload unchanged.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

2. Encrypt backup file (optional). Switch on this toggle to add a passphrase that encrypts your backed-up files for additional security. Your passphrase must contain:
- At least eight characters.
- Both upper and lower case characters.
- At least one numeric character or symbol.

Save your passphrase somewhere secure. You'll need it to decrypt and access your backed-up files later, and it can't be recovered if lost.

3. Click Save.

media.alt: ZIP file size selection dropdown

nodes.label: Backing up data to Kissflow
nodes.heading: Select data categories for backup
nodes.snippet:
Choose one or more categories to include:

- User and group data — all account user and group data.
- Transactional data — flow item details such as status, comments, and members involved.
- Audit log data — the last year's audit logs at both the flow and account levels.
- Attachments — attachments across flows, including processes and boards.

Select your categories and click Next. In the confirmation popup, verify your configuration details and click Start backup.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Not ready to start? Click Save and exit to store your configuration and resume it later instead of running the backup immediately.

From the Data backup home page, you can also edit your configuration, delete it, or view your backup history.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Backup confirmation popup showing configuration details and the Start backup button
Data backup home page with options to edit configuration, delete it, or view backup history

nodes.label: Compliance and backup history
nodes.heading: Compliance and backup history
nodes.snippet:
Compliance and backup history

Data backup is available on request. Contact Kissflow support to enable it for your account.

As a Super Admin, you manage your account's data backup — you can edit its configuration, cancel a backup that's still in the queue, and review its history.

nodes.label: Compliance and backup history
nodes.heading: Cancel a queued backup
nodes.snippet:
You can cancel a backup only while it's still in the queue, before processing starts. Once a backup is in progress, cancellation is no longer available.

1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click Account administration > Data backup.
3. While the backup shows as queued, click Cancel next to it.

nodes.label: Compliance and backup history
nodes.heading: Edit your backup configuration
nodes.snippet:
While a backup is queued or in progress, its configuration is read-only — you can view it, but not change it. Once the backup completes, the configuration becomes editable again.

1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click Account administration > Data backup.
3. Click the More options button beside Backup now, then click Edit configuration to change your backup settings, or Delete configuration to remove them entirely and stop future backups until you set up a new configuration.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Editing your configuration resets your encryption passphrase — the one you set when you first configured your backup destination. Save your previous passphrase somewhere safe before you edit — you'll still need it to open backup files created before the change, and it won't work on any backup you run afterward.

media.alt: More options icon
Data backup page with the More options menu open, showing Edit configuration, Backup history, and Delete configuration

nodes.label: Compliance and backup history
nodes.heading: Track backup progress

media.alt: Data backup page showing real-time progress through validating configuration, backup in progress, and finalizing data backup

nodes.label: Compliance and backup history
nodes.heading: View backup history
nodes.snippet:
After a backup finishes, you can review its history — the requester, storage mode, data categories included, status, and the date and time of the backup.

1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click Account administration > Data backup.
3. Click the More options button beside Backup now, then click Backup history.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: More options icon
Backup history table listing requester, storage mode, data categories, status, and date and time for completed backups
