nodes.label: Managing integrations in apps
nodes.heading: Managing integrations in apps
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

nodes.label: Managing integrations in apps
nodes.heading: Manage integrations
nodes.snippet:
To view all integrations associated with your app:

1. Open your app in the production environment.
2. Click Manage > Integrations.
3. Here, you can find a list of integrations, the connectors used, and the run history.

Connections

A connection is the authentication that lets Kissflow interact with an internal or external connector in an integration. Integration runs require valid connections before triggers and actions can be configured. Kissflow connections are based on Access key and secret token-based authentication. Most external connectors are authenticated using OAuth.

For new integrations, you must add a connection and authenticate them before turning the integration on.

To do this, click Connect now. Enter the details of your Kissflow account for authentication. You can find your account ID, account domain, Access key ID, Access key secret under My settings > Security settings > API authentication in your Kissflow account's profile.

Under Connections, there are three actions you can perform:

1. View the number of integrations using a particular connection
2. Reauthenticate a connection to ensure the integrations runs as per schedule at runtime
3. Test the connection status to ensure the integration does not fail at runtime

Run history

Every piece of data that passes through the workflow system in your application counts as an integration run. That means if your integration adds 20 items to Kissflow Board form, your integration has just performed 20 integration runs. Each time an integration run occurs, you save yourself a manual job. You can keep track of all the integration runs from the Run history of your integration page.

To access run history in production after deploying your app, go to Manage > Integrations > Run history.

All integrations

Displays a list of all integrations available in this app. You can view the status of the integration (Live/Archived) and turn it on or off from this page.

nodes.label: Managing integrations in apps
nodes.heading: Editing an integration
nodes.snippet:
To edit and manage individual integrations:

1. Click Edit app and open the app builder in the dev environment.
2. Here, click Logic > Integration from the left navigation menu.
3. You can edit, rename, duplicate, and archive an integration from here. Note that you must deploy the app for your changes to take effect.

> Note: Once your app is deployed, all integrations will be turned off by default.
>
> For your integration to work in production after deployment, you must do the following:
>
> 1. Navigate to your app in the production environment and go to Manage > Integration.
> 2. Go to Connections and click Connection now.
> 3. Authenticate your integration again with your production environment's account ID, account domain, access key details. It is imperative you don't reuse the authentication details of your development environment.
> 4. Test connection status and turn on your integration. Now, it will function as expected.
> 5. You must reauthenticate your integration's connection using your production account's details after every deployment.

nodes.label: Managing integrations
nodes.heading: Managing integrations
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Access information about all your integrations by clicking on the Explore button > Managed by me > Integrations.

nodes.label: Managing integrations
nodes.heading: Integrations
nodes.snippet:
You can find integrations in Kissflow in two different places:

- Managed by me: Displays the integrations you have created or those in which you are involved.
- Others: Displays all integrations available in your Kissflow account. Note that only Account Admins have access to these integrations.

Managing integrations

From the integrations page, you can:

- Change integration states (ON/OFF)

- Create duplicates

- Archive or unarchive integrations

- Delete integrations

- Pin frequently used integrations to the navigation

Integration states

Each integration can be in one of two states:

- ON: The integration actively checks for new data and performs actions when triggered. Note that any data received while the integration was turned off will not be processed when turned back on.
- OFF: The integration stops checking for new data and suspends all actions.

To manage an individual integration, click its name to open the integration editor.

nodes.label: Managing integrations
nodes.heading: Viewing integrations
nodes.snippet:
On the Integrations page, you can choose to view the integrations based on three views:

- List view - View the integrations in a list format.

-  Grid view - View the integrations in a grid format.

- Connector-based view - View the integrations based on the actions and triggers used within the connectors.

nodes.label: Managing integrations
nodes.heading: Searching your integrations
nodes.snippet:
You can use the search bar at the top of the Integrations page to search for specific integrations by name.

You can also sort your integrations based on the following:

- Recently created
- Recently accessed
- Ascending to descending, or vice versa
- Status - ON to OFF, or vice versa
- Archived to unarchive

nodes.label: Managing integrations
nodes.heading: Managing failed integrations
nodes.snippet:
Integrations can fail for various reasons, such as incomplete authentication or incorrect payload. It's also possible that the trigger might not receive any new events because the webhook subscription for that particular trigger has expired. To continue receiving events associated with a specific trigger or action, you must manually renew the webhook subscription. You will be notified of any failures through email and in-app notifications. If an integration has failed due to a failed webhook subscription renewal, follow these steps:

1\. Choose the integration that has failed.

2\. Change the integration's status from ON to OFF and then back to ON.

> Note:
> The subscription renewal period varies for different integrations. For most integrations, the webhook expires after 30 days.

nodes.label: Managing integrations
nodes.heading: Run history
nodes.snippet:
To open Run history, click the Explore button > Managed by me > Integrations > Run history.

The run history shows a log of all the runs based on date. It shows the unique run ID, integration name, status, and time of execution. When there is a failure, you can run the integration again by clicking the Rerun button. You can use the quick filters on the top of the page to filter history based on when it was performed, whether the run was a success or not, or by the integration name.

> Note:
>
> Integration run history will be available for the last 100 days.

When you click an individual run, you can view:

- The trigger and action names inside the integration.
- API payload with associated fields and values.
- Status of the integration run; convey the status of whether it's a success or failure.
- Error messages associated with the payload.You can use the error message when connecting with the Kissflow support team for faster resolutions.

AI troubleshooting for failed integrations

The AI-powered troubleshoot feature helps you understand why an integration failed and suggests solutions.

1. Open the failed integration from the Run history.

2. Click the AI Troubleshoot button. AI will take a few moments to analyze the error.

3. You will get a failure analysis report with three simple sections:

- Impact: A gist of the error that occurred in the integration.

- Root cause: Technical reason for the failure.

- Suggested solutions: A list of probable solutions to fix the error. It ranks each solution by severity (High, Medium, or Low) to help you prioritize the most effective one first.

> Note:
>
> AI generates this analysis. Please review the suggested solutions to ensure they fit your setup before applying them.

Bulk rerun

Using the bulk rerun feature, you can easily rerun multiple failed integration runs simultaneously, streamlining your troubleshooting process.

Using bulk rerun:

1. Navigate to Explore button > Managed by me > Integrations > Run history.

2. Review the list of integration runs. Runs are marked as Success, Failed, or In progress with corresponding color-coded status indicators.

3. Select the checkboxes next to the failed runs you want to retry. You can select up to 100 runs at a time. Only failed runs are eligible for rerun.

4. You can clear your selections by clicking Clear all at the top right.

5. Click the Rerun button at the top right of the table to start the bulk rerun process.

6. A confirmation dialog will appear. Click Continue to proceed with the rerun or Cancel to go back.

7. The status of each rerun will be updated in real-time.

8. Once complete, you'll receive a notification about the bulk rerun results.

Handling overlapping bulk reruns in integration run history

When multiple users work with integration run history simultaneously, two people may attempt to rerun the same failed integration instances. Kissflow has a process in place to manage these situations smoothly.

If you have selected some instances to rerun, but another user has already initiated the rerun process for some of those same instances just moments before you click Rerun, you will see a red alert message.

The message will inform you that some of the integration runs you selected are already being rerun or have recently been rerun by another user. It will specify how many of the selected instances fall into this category.

For example, the message might say, “8 of your 10 selected instances are already being rerun or have recently been rerun by another user.”

Editing payloads

In the event of a run failure, you can identify and edit an invalid payload that was sent, allowing you to address the issue and rerun the integration. This grants you enhanced flexibility and control over your failed integrations.To identify an invalid payload,

1. Open the failed integration from the Run history. The action associated with the error will be displayed automatically.
2. Navigate to the Output data tab, where you can locate the specific payload causing the failure. Click the Copy button to copy the payload details.

To edit the payload,

1. Go to the Input data tab within the same action and click the Edit button.
2. Edit and validate the details of the payload responsible for the failure.
3. Once you’ve made the necessary changes, click the Rerun button to run the integration again with the updated payload.

As an alternative approach, you also have the option to edit the payload directly from the Run history.

1. In the Run history, locate the specific run where the failure occurred.
2. Click the Edit payload button available for that particular run. This action will open the payload editor, allowing you to make the desired modifications to the payload details.
3. Once you’ve edited the payload to your requirements, click the Rerun button to run the integration again with the updated payload.

Refreshing the action's status

If an action fails during the status check, you don’t need to edit the payload, just rerun the failed integration.

The failure happens because the action took too long and timed out, not because of an issue with the payload. Since the payload remains unchanged, the option to edit it will be disabled, and you only need to rerun the failed step to refresh the status.

nodes.label: Managing integrations
nodes.heading: Connections
nodes.snippet:
Connections are secure authentications that allow Kissflow to communicate with other flows and third-party applications in the integration. Such authentications can be either Access key based or OAuth based. Connections can also be shared with other account members so that they can be used in other integrations.

For example, if you want to create a trigger step that will work with your Kissflow Process items, you must grant access permission to the specific Kissflow Process account. Integration runs require valid connections before triggers and actions can be configured and tested.

nodes.label: Managing integrations
nodes.heading: Create a connection
nodes.snippet:
To create a new connection, click New Connection on the Connections tab. This will prompt you to set up a new connection. You must provide a name for the connection and select the connector from the drop down. Upon completion, click Next.

Based on the connector you've selected, you will need to authenticate your connection with your Kissflow account Access key and secret or use the third-party OAuth based authentication.

For Access key based authentication, you must enter your Account domain, Access key, Account ID and click Authenticate after you enter the required values.

Keep your Access key status ON to ensure successful authentication.

When you create a connection to most third-party connectors, Kissflow redirects you to their respective pages. There, you will be prompted to grant Kissflow access to your data, which is based on OAuth authentication.

If everything goes well, a successful connection is established.

nodes.label: Managing integrations
nodes.heading: Testing your connections
nodes.snippet:
To test if the connection between a connector and Kissflow is still active, click the Test connection status on the connection.

There are two possible cases after you test your connection. They are:

- Test successful: The connection is active.
- Reconnection needed: The connection is no longer active. You will need to reconnect with your connector.

nodes.label: Managing integrations
nodes.heading: Renaming your connections
nodes.snippet:
If you have multiple Kissflow accounts for the same connector linked to Kissflow, you can rename them to easily identify each of them in your Kissflow account.

- Go to the Connections tab.
- Click the More options button at the end of the connection name and select Rename. The new connection name will be auto-saved soon after entering the new connection name.

nodes.label: Managing integrations
nodes.heading: Re-authenticate your connections
nodes.snippet: The connections between a connector and Kissflow may expire if the Access key and secret are suspended/deleted or due to a connection error. To re-authenticate with a connector account, click the Re-authenticate connection  button. You must re-authenticate your connection with a new Access key and secret or reconfigure your OAuth-based connection.

nodes.label: Managing integrations
nodes.heading: Sharing connections
nodes.snippet:
Connections can also be shared with other account users. In this manner, a shared connection can be used in integration steps that you did not create. Connections can be viewed in either a list or a segment view. They can also be filtered to see connections created by you, connections shared with you, or a combined view.

You can also view the list of integrations that the connection is a part of by clicking the integration count.

To share a connection:

- Go to the Connections tab.

- Click the More options button at the end of the connection name and select Share.

- Then, click \+ Add members button to add members and assign them member or admin permissions.

Connection role

Permissions

Admin

Can use and modify the connection in any integration steps

Member

Can only use the connection in any integration steps

Connections used within an integration are not inherited unless they are intentionally shared with you.

nodes.label: Migrating a connector to its latest version
nodes.heading: Migrating a connector to its latest version
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Kissflow automatically tries to update to the latest version of a connector through migration whenever a new version is available. However, in certain cases where migration is not possible, the older version of the connector will be marked for deprecation. This guide will help you identify when a new connector version is available and how to update to it.

nodes.label: Migrating a connector to its latest version
nodes.heading: Identifying a new connector version
nodes.snippet:
There are three different ways of identifying connectors scheduled for deprecation.

1. Paying attention to deprecation notifications in your Kissflow account.

2. Looking for the Deprecated tab on the integration home page.

3. Checking the Kissflow Community for announcements about new connector version releases.

nodes.label: Migrating a connector to its latest version
nodes.heading: Frequency of notifications for connector deprecation
nodes.snippet:
To ensure you have ample time to update your integrations, apps, or portals, we send out notifications about connector deprecation at key intervals:

1. Initial deprecation notice: You will receive the first notification as soon as a connector is scheduled for deprecation. This gives you the maximum amount of time to plan and implement necessary updates.

2. One week reminder: A second notification will be sent one week before the scheduled deprecation date. This serves as a reminder to finalize any pending updates.

3. Day of deprecation: On the actual day of deprecation, a final notification will be sent. This alerts you that the connector is no longer supported and any integrations using it may cease to function properly.

nodes.label: Migrating a connector to its latest version
nodes.heading: Migrating to the latest version
nodes.snippet:
1. On the integration home page, click the "Deprecated" tab.

2. Navigate to the integration builder by clicking on the particular integration that you would like to migrate.

3. View the help text explaining the deprecation status of the action.

4. Click the action and view the configuration section on the right slider panel.

5. Turn off the integration for re-configuration.

6. Click the Change action option and choose the appropriate new action.

7. Upon selecting the new action, a modal window will open with two sections:

1. The left section is read-only, containing the deprecated version's connection details, configuration details, and output details.

2. The right section contains the selected version's details, where you need to start by creating a connection, proceed with configuration, testing the action, and end with output.

8. If you exit the modal while configuring, your configuration will be saved. You can reopen the modal by clicking the action card.

9. Once the output is tested, you will be moved from the modal to the regular right panel.

10. You can now turn ON the integration with the updated connector.

The above steps are similar for changing a trigger, but you will not have to reconfigure the trigger, and you will not see the comparison window used to configure the fields in an action.

By following these steps, you can easily identify when a new connector version is available and update your integrations to use the latest version, ensuring optimal performance and avoiding any issues with deprecated versions.

> Note:
>
> If you are using triggers of a deprecated connector, we will force turn off the integration to avoid usage of the deprecated trigger. Please be aware that any trigger data generated after the integration is turned OFF will not be stored and cannot be retrieved later.

nodes.label: Renaming, Duplicating, and archiving app integrations
nodes.heading: Renaming, Duplicating, and archiving app integrations
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

nodes.label: Renaming, Duplicating, and archiving app integrations
nodes.heading: Renaming an integration
nodes.snippet:
If you want to rename the name and description of your integration, follow the steps below:

1. Open the integration in a tab, click the More options button and then click Rename.
2. Modify the default name and description of the integration.
3. Click Rename.

nodes.label: Renaming, Duplicating, and archiving app integrations
nodes.heading: Duplicating an integration
nodes.snippet:
If you want to make an exact copy of your integration, you can duplicate it. Duplicated integrations will have the same set of trigger and action steps with configuration data.

1. Inside the Integration card, click the More options button  and then click Duplicate.
2. The default name of the new integration is Copy of <original integration's name>. You can change the name.
3. Click Duplicate.

nodes.label: Renaming, Duplicating, and archiving app integrations
nodes.heading: Archiving an integration
nodes.snippet:
You can archive and delete any integration in your application when you don’t need it anymore. An integration can be deleted only after it’s archived. When you archive an integration, all its active workflows will stop and remain suspended till it's unarchived.

However, if you are an Admin or member of the application, you can access the Integrations page to unarchive or delete them.

To archive an integration, click the Integrations page, click the specific integration card > More options button > Archive.

When the archived integration is shown, click the integration and then click Unarchive. You can also permanently delete the integration and all its step configurations by clicking Delete.

An alternative way to Rename, Duplicate, and Archive an integration is to click Logic > Integration in the left navigation menu and click the more options menu beside the integration's name.

nodes.label: Run script connector
nodes.heading: Run script connector
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

nodes.label: Run script connector
nodes.heading: What is the run script connector?
nodes.snippet:
The Run script connector allows you to run JavaScript code in response to a trigger or a previous action step in your App's integration. The JavaScript code interacts with the data from these steps and generates an output that the remaining actions can consume. This connector does not support any trigger event for now.

For example, consider you have a board form for tracking externally reported events, and you want to individually contact customers through email only after the incident ticket is closed. For this to happen, the board and email connector must communicate with one another. The Run script connector maintains a constant communication channel by retrieving only the necessary email addresses from the board and forwarding them to the Email connector action step. The code connector's output will be an array of objects containing email strings. When the integration is enabled, separate email notifications are sent when incidents are closed.

Actions supported in Run script connector

Action

Description

Run JavaScript code

This action runs an inserted JavaScript in response to a trigger from any of the previous steps.

nodes.label: Run script connector
nodes.heading: Configuring Run script connector
nodes.snippet:
1. In your Kissflow account, open the app builder. Click the left navigation menu and click Logic > Integration > + icon to create an integration. Provide a name and description for your integration.
2. In the editor, select a trigger step. You can also use the search to find a trigger directly. For instance, use a webhook from a third-party application or a trigger from one of the flows in Kissflow.
3. Click the Add button  to add an action. Search for Run script connector and select Run JavaScript code as the action.
4. Click Edit on the JavaScript code editor, the full-screen editor opens.
- Input Data (Left panel): Configure custom input data by mapping fields from previous triggers or actions. You can apply formulas to transform this data before it enters your code.
- Code editor: The main workspace where you can draft your script. Write custom vanilla JavaScript from scratch or use the RunJS assistant to generate the code for you
- RunJS assistant (Right panel): The interactive chat interface for generating and refining code.

How to use the RunJS assistant

Adding input data

You can pass dynamic data into your script by defining key-value pairs in the Input data section. These fields map values from previous steps (triggers or actions) to a global inputData object. For example, if you map a field as email, you can access it within your code using _inputData.email_.

Generating code from scratch

In the RunJS assistant chat on the right, describe the logic you want to achieve (For example, _Remove all duplicate emails and return a unique list_).

- The RunJS assistant will generate the code block for you.

- It can automatically identify and create necessary Input data fields or use your existing ones.

- If you are satisfied with the result, click Insert code to paste it directly into the center editor. Use the Undo and Redo controls in the code editor at any time to navigate through your recent code changes and restore previous versions.

> Note:
> To make your prompt more precise, type / to trigger a dropdown of available fields and configured input data. Selecting data here ensures the assistant incorporates your specific data into the generated script.
>
>

Iterative edits

If the generated code needs any changes, click Reply on the specific chat bubble to provide additional instructions.

Refining existing code

To edit existing code in the editor that needs optimization or changes:

- Highlight the specific lines of code in the code editor.

- Click the Refine button that appears.

- The code will be pulled into the RunJS assistant chat. Type your prompt (For example, _Refine this code so that it also converts all email addresses to lowercase before deduplicating, ensuring 'User@Email.com' and 'user@email.com' are treated as the same_) to modify it.

- Select the code to be replaced again and click Replace code below the code widget to update the editor with the new version.

Testing and saving

Before finalizing your integration, you can verify your logic within the editor. Click Test action at the top right to run the code against your sample data.

If test is successful, close the code editor and you'll be taken back to the integration configuration page. Choose when this step happens and click Next.

Finally, review the output and click Done.

> Note:
>
> - The Run JavaScript action executes as an isolated, third-party script. It cannot access Kissflow's built-in system variables, for example, kf.account._id, kf.context, form fields, or user info.
>
> Any data you need, such as an Account ID or field value, must be explicitly declared or passed into the script as an input. This isolation is by design for security and consistency.
>
> - Similarly, App variables are not supported inside the Run JavaScript action.

nodes.label: Run script connector
nodes.heading: Supported functions
nodes.snippet: The JavaScript Software Development Kit (SDK) for Kissflow Low-code includes all the supported functions and code snippets you can incorporate into your application while integrating with other services.

nodes.label: Run script connector
nodes.heading: Using the connector
nodes.snippet: After configuring your connector, turn ON the toggle button in the upper-right corner of the editor to activate the integration. When the configured action is triggered, the output data from the code connector is sent as input to the integration's subsequent steps.

nodes.label: Troubleshooting API integration issues
nodes.heading: Troubleshooting API integration issues
nodes.snippet:
API integrations in Kissflow allow you to connect with external systems and automate workflows. However, issues may occasionally arise due to authentication errors, misconfigurations, or endpoint problems.

This article provides a structured guide to diagnosing and resolving common API integration issues in Kissflow, including specific error types such as An unexpected error occurred and socket hang up.

nodes.label: Troubleshooting API integration issues
nodes.heading: Initial checks for issues in API integrations
nodes.snippet:
Check and reconnect the authentication

- Verify that the integration’s authentication is correctly configured.

- Reconnect credentials if triggers fail or items are rejected.

- Ensure the same authentication method (OAuth, API key, and so on.) is used consistently across all connection steps.

- Confirm you are connected to the correct environment (production vs. testing).

Verify configuration and settings

- Double-check that configurations align between Kissflow and the external system.

- Validate all linkage, mapping, and settings across the integration.

- If some trigger stages succeed but others fail, carefully recheck each step for minor inconsistencies.

Rebuild the integration

- If error messages are vague or troubleshooting does not resolve the issue, rebuild the integration from scratch.

- Rebuilding helps eliminate compounded configuration issues.

- This is especially useful when initial triggers succeed but later steps consistently fail.

nodes.label: Troubleshooting API integration issues
nodes.heading: Troubleshooting common API errors
nodes.snippet:
General API errors

This error is often seen during third-party API calls. To resolve it:

- Validate the API request

- Check the JSON payload for syntax errors.

- Confirm the endpoint (URL) is correct.

- Test the request in Postman to verify it works.

- Confirm header configurations

- Ensure all required headers are included.

- For example, if an authorization header is needed, include it with the correct value.

- Check API availability

- Confirm the external system is online and able to accept requests.

- Gather error details

- Collect error messages, payloads, and configurations.

- Share details with Kissflow support for deeper troubleshooting.

Socket hang up errors

A socket hang up error occurs when Kissflow cannot establish or maintain a connection with an external endpoint (such as a custom API or script).

How to diagnose and resolve

1. Review logs

- Check Kissflow execution logs for failed requests.

- If an external script (such as, Google Apps Script) is used, review its logs as well.

2. Isolate the source

- If failures are external, contact the endpoint or script provider.

- If the problem appears internal, escalate to Kissflow support with detailed logs.

3. Handle transient errors

- If rerunning the integration works, monitor for recurring failures.

- Repeated issues may indicate endpoint instability.

4. Resolve known issues

- Fix bugs in custom scripts (for example, updating Google Apps Script logic).

- Work with the endpoint provider to improve availability.

- Retry failed runs for temporary issues.

nodes.label: Troubleshooting API integration issues
nodes.heading: Best practices for stable integrations
nodes.snippet:
- Monitor execution logs regularly to catch and address issues early.

- Update external APIs, scripts, and services to reduce compatibility issues.

- Document recurring patterns in failures for faster root cause identification.

- Escalate persistent issues to the endpoint provider or Kissflow support with complete error logs.

nodes.label: Agreement management
nodes.heading: Agreement management
nodes.snippet:
Agreement Management is the foundation of business operations, formalizing everything from hiring and sales contracts to procurement and legal approvals. Manual, paper-based signature processes are slow, costly, and lack security. Integrations are essential for modernizing this function, connecting core business workflows directly with trusted e-signature platforms like Adobe Sign and DocuSign.

This automation streamlines the entire lifecycle of an agreement, ensuring a fast, secure, and fully auditable process that accelerates business and enhances compliance.

nodes.label: Agreement management
nodes.heading: Connectors in this vertical
nodes.snippet:
Application

Description

Adobe Sign

An enterprise-grade e-signature solution from Adobe, trusted for its robust security and deep integration into the Adobe Document Cloud.

DocuSign

A leading e-signature platform known for its ease of use and powerful template management capabilities, helping organizations automate the agreement process.

HTTP

Apart from the pre-built actions available in Kissflow Integrations for agreement management, there may be scenarios where you need to perform a custom action. In such cases, you can use the HTTP connector to configure API calls from scratch to interact with any service that exposes a REST API.

nodes.label: Agreement management
nodes.heading: Integration use cases with Kissflow
nodes.snippet:
Here are practical, scenario-based examples of how Kissflow can trigger actions in Adobe Sign and DocuSign to solve common business problems.

Adobe Sign

Business goal

Trigger (in Kissflow)

Action (in Adobe Sign)

Detailed scenario

Key benefit

Streamline new hire onboarding

A new hire form completes its final approval.

Create an agreement using a template.

After approvals, the integration automatically generates an offer letter using a template and sends it to the new employee for signature.

Accelerates the hiring process and improves the candidate experience with a fast, professional offer process.

Secure vendor quotation approval

A vendor submits a request with a quotation document.

Create an agreement using a document.

The integration takes the vendor's uploaded quotation and routes it to the procurement manager for their electronic signature so that the process can proceed.

Streamlines procurement cycles with a secure, auditable sign-off process for unique vendor documents.

DocuSign

Business goal

Trigger (in Kissflow)

Action (in DocuSign)

Detailed scenario

Key benefit

Automate O_ffer letter_ process

The status of an onboarding form changes to _Offer approved_.

Create an agreement using a template.

An offer letter is automatically generated from a DocuSign template, populated with the new hire's information, and sent for signature. The status is tracked back in Kissflow.

Creates a seamless, end-to-end process from approval to signature with complete visibility for HR.

> Note
>
> All italicized text in the above scenarios represents sample data for reference.

nodes.label: Communication and collaboration
nodes.heading: Communication and collaboration
nodes.snippet: Effective communication and collaboration are the lifelines of any modern organization. This vertical focuses on the tools that connect teams, customers, and systems in real time. Integrations are essential to break down silos, ensuring that notifications, alerts, and conversations happen in the right context and at the right moment. By connecting communication platforms with core business applications, companies can automate critical alerts, streamline project kick-offs, and create seamless channels for customer support and engagement, ultimately leading to a more responsive and aligned organization.

nodes.label: Communication and collaboration
nodes.heading: Connectors in this vertical
nodes.snippet:
Application

Description

Twilio

A customer engagement platform that enables personalized, real-time communication like SMS and WhatsApp messages to be embedded directly into business workflows.

Microsoft Teams

A unified collaboration platform that combines workplace chat, video meetings, file storage, and application integration for seamless teamwork.

Outlook

A widely used personal information manager from Microsoft provides email, calendar, and contact management services central to business communication.

HTTP

Apart from the pre-built actions available in Kissflow Integrations for communication and collaboration, there may be scenarios where you need to perform a custom action. In such cases, you can use the HTTP connector to configure API calls from scratch to interact with any service that exposes a REST API.

nodes.label: Communication and collaboration
nodes.heading: Integration use cases with Kissflow
nodes.snippet:
Here are practical, scenario-based examples of how Kissflow can trigger actions in Twilio, Microsoft Teams, and Outlook to solve common business problems.

Twilio

Business goal

Trigger (in Kissflow)

Action (in Twilio)

Detailed scenario

Key benefit

Proactive delivery updates

A delivery driver marks an order _Out for delivery_ in a logistics app.

Send an SMS.

An SMS is sent to the customer: _Your package is on its way! Track it here: <link>._

Improves customer experience and reduces support inquiries about order status.

Enhance guest welcome experience

A hotel guest finishes mobile check-in via a form.

Send a WhatsApp message.

A WhatsApp message is sent: _Welcome to Seaside Resort! Your room is ready (#512). Reply ‘YES’ when you’re on property._

Provides a modern, convenient communication channel and a personalized guest experience.

Microsoft Teams

Business goal

Trigger (in Kissflow)

Action (in Microsoft Teams)

Detailed scenario

Key benefit

Automate project workspace setup

A new client project is approved in a project-initiation workflow.

Create a channel.

Kissflow instantly creates a channel named _Proj-Acme-2025_ under the _Client Projects_ team, pre-setting owners.

Ensures project teams have a dedicated collaboration space instantly, standardizing project setup.

Escalate critical delays

A procurement request breaches its deadline.

Send a channel message.

The flow posts a message in the _#supply-chain channel: PO #7843 is delayed by 5 days. Click for details._

Provides real-time visibility into critical issues for the relevant team, prompting faster action.

Communicate sensitive HR decisions

An employee’s vacation request is rejected in an HR process.

Send a chat message.

Kissflow sends a private chat to the requester: _Your PTO for Jun 3–5 was declined by your manager. Please review comments_.

Delivers private and timely notifications for sensitive information directly to the employee.

Outlook

Business goal

Trigger (in Outlook)

Action (in Kissflow)

Detailed scenario

Key benefit

Streamline IT support ticketing

A new email is received at _itsupport@yourcompany.com_.

Create a new item.

An email sent to IT support automatically creates a new support ticket in a Kissflow board, mapping the subject, body, and sender.

Lowers the barrier for employees to submit requests and centralizes all tickets in one system.

nodes.label: Finance and accounting
nodes.heading: Finance and accounting
nodes.snippet: Finance and accounting platforms are the backbone of any business, managing cash flow, tracking expenses, invoicing customers, and ensuring regulatory compliance. The accuracy and timeliness of financial data are paramount. Integrations are crucial for maintaining this accuracy by connecting accounting software with other operational systems like sales (CRM), procurement, and HR. This creates a single source of truth for financial data, automating tedious processes like invoice creation, expense reporting, and customer billing. By doing so, businesses can close their books faster, reduce the risk of human error, and gain real-time visibility into their financial health.

nodes.label: Finance and accounting
nodes.heading: Connectors in this vertical
nodes.snippet:
Application

Description

QuickBooks

A comprehensive accounting software package geared mainly toward small and medium-sized businesses, handling invoicing, bill management, expense tracking, and financial reporting.

FreshBooks

An accounting software focused on service-based small businesses and freelancers, specializing in invoicing, expense management, and time tracking.

Expensify

A specialized expense management system that automates every step of the expense reporting process, from receipt scanning to reimbursement.

HTTP

Apart from the pre-built actions available in Kissflow Integrations for HR management, there may be scenarios where you need to perform a custom action. In such cases, you can use the HTTP connector to configure API calls from scratch to interact with any service that exposes a REST API.

nodes.label: Finance and accounting
nodes.heading: Integration use cases with Kissflow
nodes.snippet:
Here are practical, scenario-based examples of how Kissflow can trigger actions in QuickBooks, FreshBooks, and Expensify to solve common business problems.

QuickBooks

Business goal

Trigger (in Kissflow)

Action (in QuickBooks)

Detailed scenario

Key benefit

Streamline customer onboarding

Sales approves a new client.

Create a customer.

When sales approves a new client in Kissflow, the integration creates a customer record in QuickBooks with billing and contact info for invoicing.

Ensures timely and accurate invoicing from the first day without manual data entry.

Maintain accurate client data

A customer updates their details in a self-service portal.

Update a customer.

The QuickBooks customer record is updated instantly if a customer updates their address or payment terms in Kissflow’s portal.

Guarantees that invoices and communications are always sent to the correct address.

Automate invoicing

Order fulfillment is confirmed.

Create an invoice.

After order fulfillment is confirmed in a Kissflow workflow, the integration creates an invoice for the shipped products in QuickBooks.

Accelerates the billing cycle and improves cash flow by issuing invoices immediately.

Simplify payment reconciliation

A payment is received and verified.

Create a payment.

When a payment is recorded in Kissflow, the integration automatically records it against the correct invoice in QuickBooks.

Reduces manual reconciliation effort and provides a real-time view of paid invoices.

Manage vendor payables

A supplier’s purchase order is approved.

Create a bill.

Upon receipt of a supplier’s PO approval in Kissflow, the integration creates a bill in QuickBooks for the vendor to process payment.

Streamlines the accounts payable process and ensures vendors are paid on time.

Automate vendor setup

A new supplier is approved.

Create a vendor.

New suppliers approved in a Kissflow procurement workflow are instantly added as vendors in QuickBooks with tax and banking info.

Centralizes vendor information and makes it immediately available for billing.

Record point-of-sale transactions

An instant retail payment is processed.

Create a sales receipt.

For instant payments processed via a Kissflow POS app, a sales receipt is created in QuickBooks to record the sale and payment together.

Keeps financial records perfectly in sync with daily sales activities.

Standardize sales quoting

A sales quote is approved.

Create an estimate.

During sales quoting, Kissflow creates an estimate in QuickBooks from the approved quote, ready to be converted to an invoice later.

Bridges the gap between sales quoting and financial tracking.

Streamline the procurement cycle

The procurement team approves a new order request.

Create a purchase order.

The integration creates a purchase order in QuickBooks with vendor details, items, and quantities, triggering the vendor to process the order.

Automates the purchasing process, reduces manual errors, and provides a clear audit trail.

Simplify expense reimbursement

A travel reimbursement form is approved.

Create an expense.

The workflow creates an expense record in QuickBooks, attaches receipt details, and allocates the cost to the correct department budget.

Ensures accurate and timely reimbursement while maintaining precise departmental budget tracking.

Maintain product catalog sync

The product team adds a new SKU to a product catalog form.

Create an item.

An item is automatically created in QuickBooks with price and inventory settings, ensuring sales and finance have the latest product info.

Guarantees pricing and product consistency across all business functions.

Empower support with data

A customer service rep receives a client call.

Find a customer.

By entering the client's name in Kissflow, the system searches QuickBooks and retrieves their contact and transaction history.

Improves first-call resolution by giving support agents instant access to financial history.

Enable quick invoice lookup

The finance team needs to check the status of an invoice.

Find an invoice.

They input the invoice number into Kissflow, which searches QuickBooks and displays its status, payment history, and balance.

Provides immediate access to invoice details without needing to switch applications.

Facilitate vendor management

The procurement team reviews a vendor’s performance.

Find a vendor.

They enter the vendor's name in Kissflow to search QuickBooks for past transactions, payment terms, and performance metrics.

Supports data-driven decisions by providing a complete view of vendor history.

Provide real-time inventory data

An inventory manager checks product availability.

Find Products.

They search in Kissflow to retrieve product details from QuickBooks, including stock levels, pricing, and supplier information.

Helps prevent stockouts and informs purchasing decisions with up-to-date inventory data.

Support financial audits

The accounting team needs to review specific accounts.

Find accounts.

They input the account name into Kissflow, which searches QuickBooks and displays transaction history and current balances.

Streamlines the audit process by making account information easily accessible.

Ensure tax compliance

The tax department prepares for a filing.

Find tax codes.

They search for tax codes in Kissflow, which retrieves the information from QuickBooks to ensure accurate calculations and compliance.

Reduces compliance risk by providing easy access to correct tax information.

Speeds up the billing cycle for regular customers and eliminates manual invoice creation.

nodes.label: HR management
nodes.heading: HR management
nodes.snippet: Human Resources is the core of any organization, managing the entire employee lifecycle from recruitment and onboarding to development and offboarding. The efficiency and quality of HR processes directly impact employee satisfaction and company culture. Integrations are vital for modern HR, connecting systems like Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) with internal workflow platforms like Kissflow. This automation streamlines repetitive tasks, eliminates manual data entry, reduces the risk of errors in critical documents like offer letters, and ensures a smooth, professional experience for both candidates and employees.

Accelerates the hiring process, reduces manual errors in offer creation, and ensures a professional, timely candidate experience.

nodes.label: Identity and access management (IAM)
nodes.heading: Identity and access management (IAM)
nodes.snippet: Identity and Access Management is the security foundation of the modern enterprise, ensuring that the right individuals have access to the right resources at the right times, and for the right reasons. Integrations are the cornerstone of an effective IAM strategy, connecting HR systems and internal workflows with identity providers like Okta. By automating user lifecycle management, provisioning, audits, and de-provisioning, organizations can enhance security, ensure compliance, and provide a seamless access experience for employees from their first day to their last.

Streamline access audits

Get user details.

The workflow retrieves that user’s profile from Okta: _status, groups, last login_ and displays it in the Kissflow form.

Speeds up security and compliance audits by providing instant access to user data within the audit workflow.

Secure employee offboarding

A manager completes an _Employee exit_ checklist.

Deactivate a user.

Kissflow instantly deactivates the departing user in Okta, blocking all system access while downstream off-boarding steps continue.

Mitigates security risks by ensuring immediate and reliable revocation of access for departing employees.

nodes.label: Marketing automation
nodes.heading: Marketing automation
nodes.snippet: Marketing automation platforms are the engines of modern digital strategy. They empower businesses to communicate with customers and prospects at scale, delivering personalized messages at the right time. Integrations are the fuel for this engine. By connecting marketing platforms with other business systems like CRMs, e-commerce stores, and event management tools, companies can create a seamless, 360-degree view of their audience. This allows for powerful automation, from lead nurturing and customer segmentation to abandoned cart reminders and post-purchase follow-ups, ensuring that data flows freely and marketing efforts are efficient and effective.

nodes.label: Project management
nodes.heading: Project management
nodes.snippet: Project management platforms are the command center for modern teamwork, ensuring that projects are delivered on time, within budget, and to the required standard. The core challenge is maintaining visibility, alignment, and momentum across teams. Integrations are the key to creating a connected project ecosystem. By linking project management tools with other business processes, organizations can automate task creation, synchronize status updates, and ensure that stakeholders have a real-time view of progress, turning fragmented workflows into a cohesive operation.

Business goal

Trigger (in Kissflow)

Action (in Monday.com)

Detailed scenario

Key benefit

Automate project setup

A new project is initiated.

Standardizes project setup and ensures consistency across all new initiatives.

Adapt to evolving needs

Allows project boards to adapt quickly to changing team requirements.

Improves project clarity and organization by structuring tasks into phases.

Streamline task creation

Break down complex tasks

Improves task clarity and helps track progress on a more granular level.

Provides clear visibility into the completion of smaller steps within a larger task.

Maintain naming consistency

Basecamp

Business goal

Trigger (in Kissflow)

Action (in Basecamp)

Detailed scenario

Key benefit

Generate leadership dashboards

A scheduled workflow runs every Monday morning.

Find all projects.

A workflow retrieves the complete list of active Basecamp projects to build a fresh status summary board in Kissflow for leadership.

Provides automated, regular visibility into the entire project portfolio for executive review.

Provide quick context

A help-desk agent selects a project in a request form.

Find a project.

The flow looks up the specific project in Basecamp, pulling its ID and current to-dos to embed in the Kissflow ticket.

Empowers support agents with immediate project context to resolve issues faster.

Custom trigger

A custom trigger allows you to use a single integration to handle multiple, related events. For example, instead of separate integrations for employee onboarding and offboarding, a custom trigger can listen for changes in an _Employee maste_r dataset. When a new employee is added or an existing one's status changes to _Terminated_, the same integration can trigger the appropriate onboarding or offboarding workflow in Kissflow, simplifying management and maintenance.

Kissflow Board

Pattern

Scenario

Key benefit

Automated subtask creation

In a _Customer support_ board, a master ticket often requires action from multiple departments. When a ticket is created, the system can automatically create a subitem for each relevant team such as, IT, billing, technical.

Ensures complex issues are correctly distributed to all necessary teams without manual intervention.

Synchronized status updates

If the status of a parent ticket is changed to _Cancelled_, the workflow can automatically update a subitem, looping through all related sub-tickets and setting their status to _Cancelled_ as well.

Maintains data integrity and prevents teams from working on tasks related to a cancelled request.

Automatic data cleansing

In a sales CRM board, you want to remove invalid leads. When a new lead is added, a condition can check if the email is blacklisted or if the phone number is empty. If true, the system will delete an item.

Keeps the sales pipeline clean and ensures reps only work on valid, actionable leads.

Kissflow Process

Create and submit items in bulk

In many organizations, teams need to initiate multiple process items at once, such as submitting dozens of purchase requests from a single spreadsheet. Instead of manual entry, Kissflow's create and submit items in bulk action can automate this. An integration can be scheduled to run daily, read all rows from a dataset (such as, an Excel file of purchase requests), and automatically create and submit an item in the _Purchase request_ process for each row, mapping the spreadsheet columns to the form fields. This saves hours of repetitive work and eliminates data entry errors.

nodes.label: Sales
nodes.heading: Sales
nodes.snippet: Sales is the engine of business growth, focused on converting leads into customers and nurturing long-term relationships. Integrations are the high-octane fuel for this engine, connecting CRMs like Salesforce and Salesmate with all other parts of the business—from marketing and e-commerce to project management and finance. By automating the flow of data, sales teams can eliminate manual tasks, gain a 360-degree view of the customer, accelerate the sales cycle, and spend more time doing what they do best: selling.

nodes.label: Utility and general
nodes.heading: Utility and general
nodes.snippet: The utility and general vertical are not tied to a specific business function like sales or marketing; they provide foundational, versatile tools that enhance automated workflow. These connectors are the essential building blocks for creating more intelligent, customized, and robust integrations. They handle universal tasks like controlling timing, converting data, and creating custom connections, allowing organizations to solve unique challenges and fine-tune their processes with precision across any department.

Custom action

Apart from the pre-built actions available in Kissflow Integrations for miscellaneous purposes, there may be scenarios where you need to perform a custom action. In such cases, you can use the HTTP connector to configure API calls from scratch to interact with any service that exposes a REST API.
