- Using board layouts

nodes.label: Creating and managing subitems
nodes.heading: Creating and managing subitems
nodes.snippet:
Creating and managing subitems

Subitems help you break down complex tasks into smaller pieces and track them efficiently. Like an item, a subitem has an assignee, a state, a start date, and a due date. Breaking down items into subitems helps you estimate the scope and duration of a task, which can significantly improve turnaround time.

nodes.label: Creating and managing subitems
nodes.heading: Create a subitem
nodes.snippet:
1. Open the item from the board and click Subitems in the right navigation strip.
2. Click Add subitem.
3. Type a title for the subitem.

You can also create subitems in other ways:

- Paste text — copy a list of text and click the Paste subitems button inside the item. You can paste up to 20 subitems at a time.
- Generate with AI — let AI suggest subitems related to your main item, then click Generate to create them.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

While creating a subitem, you can set its state, assignee, and start and due dates right from the item form — or configure them later. Click a subitem to open its form and add a description, which gives other users more context.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

An item can have up to 100 subitems.

To rename a subitem, hover over it and click the Edit title button, or open the subitem and rename it in its form.

media.alt: Subitems tab of an item showing the AI option to generate subitems
Animation of creating a subitem and setting its state, assignee, and dates

nodes.label: Creating and managing subitems
nodes.heading: Manage a subitem
nodes.snippet:
- Set the state — choose a state from the dropdown to indicate progress: Not started (the default), In progress, On hold, or Done.
- Set an assignee — click the Set an assignee button and choose a user. Restricted members can be assigned to a subitem only if they're the assignee of the item.
- Set dates — click the Set start and due dates button and select the dates.
- Copy the URL — click the More options button > Copy URL to share or reference the subitem.
- Clone — click the More options button > Clone. The clone inherits all the properties and information of the original.
- Delete — click the More options button > Delete.
- Reorder — drag and drop subitems into the positions you want.

nodes.label: Creating and managing subitems
nodes.heading: Find subitems in a board
nodes.snippet:
- Search — enter the name of the subitem.
- Expand or collapse — use the Expand subitems / Collapse subitems button to show or hide all subitems in the board, or expand a single item to see just its subitems.
- Filter — enable Apply filter to subitems in the filter pane to include subitems in your filtered results.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Filter pane with the Apply filter to subitems option enabled

nodes.label: Creating and managing subitems
nodes.heading: Convert and move subitems
nodes.snippet:
Sometimes a subitem grows into work that needs to be tracked separately, or scattered items belong under a common parent. You can convert in both directions, and move subitems between items.

Convert a subitem to an item

Click the More options button on the subitem > Convert to item. In the dialog, map the title (mandatory) and description (optional) of the subitem to fields in the board form, then click Continue.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Convert an item to a subitem

Open the item, click the More options button at the top right of the form, and select Convert to subitem. Select the target item, map the title and description fields, and click Continue. If you convert an item that has subitems, the item and its subitems all become subitems of the selected item.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Move a subitem to another item

Click the More options button on the subitem > Move to other item, then select the target item.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

If an item's assignee is a restricted member, only items assigned to that restricted member are listed when an admin or member converts items to subitems or moves subitems.

media.alt: Animation of converting a subitem into an item by mapping its title and description fields
Animation of converting an item into a subitem of another item
Animation of moving a subitem from one item to another

nodes.label: Creating and managing subitems
nodes.heading: What's next

- Creating and managing items
- Collaborating on items
- Adding advanced filters using AI (Enterprise)

nodes.label: Exporting items from a board
nodes.heading: Exporting items from a board
nodes.snippet:
Exporting items from a board

You can export items from a board to a CSV file. The file is emailed to the email address registered with your Kissflow account.

Exporting helps you share information about items with people who may not have access to Kissflow, or move data into other applications.

nodes.label: Exporting items from a board
nodes.heading: Export items
nodes.snippet:
1. In any layout — list, kanban, matrix, or timeline — click the More options button in the upper-right corner of the layout and select Export.

The Export option is only available to board admins and members with export permission — if you don't see it, ask your board admin.

2. In the Export data dialog, confirm that only the fields shown in the current layout will be exported, and click Export.
3. Download the exported CSV file from your email.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Animation of exporting items from a board through the More options menu and the Export data dialog

nodes.label: Exporting items from a board
nodes.heading: What gets exported
nodes.snippet:
The export includes only the fields visible in your current layout — hidden fields are not exported. If you have applied a filter or sorted the items, the same conditions apply to the exported data. For example, if a filter is active, only the filtered items are exported.

These field types are never included, even if visible:

- Lookup
- Image
- Signature
- Attachment
- Checklist

Remote lookup fields are exported as text values — unlike standard Lookup fields, they aren't excluded.

Fields inside tables (child items) are exported too, but as a separate CSV file. If your export includes table data, you'll receive two files by email: one for the item's own fields and one for its table rows.

Other item details, such as notes, activity logs, and status transitions, are not included in the export.

Currency fields appear as two columns in the exported file: one for the currency code and one for the value.

nodes.label: Exporting items from a board
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:
- Importing items from a CSV file
- Notes, status logs, and activity logs in boards
- Creating and managing subitems
- Adding advanced filters using AI (Enterprise)
- Using board layouts

nodes.label: Adding advanced filters using AI
nodes.heading: Adding advanced filters using AI
nodes.snippet:
Adding advanced filters using AI

Instead of manually building filter conditions, you can describe what you want to see in plain language and let AI build the filter for you.

media.alt: AI-powered advanced filters - Overview video

nodes.label: Adding advanced filters using AI
nodes.heading: Generate a filter from a prompt
nodes.snippet:
1. Click the Filter button on your board.
2. In the filter pane that opens, click Advanced filter.
3. In the text box under Generate advanced filter using AI, type your filtering criteria in simple, plain language. For example: _My high priority assigned items._
4. Click Generate. AI creates the corresponding filter conditions in the section below. You can also add or adjust conditions manually.
5. Once you're satisfied with the conditions, click Apply to see your filtered results.

{/* TODO: screenshot — filter pane with the Generate advanced filter using AI text box and generated conditions */}

To include subitems in your filtered results, enable Apply filter to subitems in the filter pane.

nodes.label: Adding advanced filters using AI
nodes.heading: Summarize and regenerate filters
nodes.snippet:
Click Filter summary to get an AI-generated summary describing what your filter conditions represent. You can use this summary as a base for new prompts:

1. Click Edit next to the filter summary.
2. Update the summary text to reflect your new requirement.
3. Click Regenerate to make AI create a new set of filter conditions.

For example, if the summary says _This filter shows items with priority High that are assigned to the current user_, you can edit it to say _This filter shows items with priority Critical that are assigned to the current user_ and regenerate the filter.

nodes.label: Adding advanced filters using AI
nodes.heading: Example prompts
nodes.snippet:
| To see... | You can type... |
|---|---|
| High-priority items assigned to you | "My high priority assigned items" |
| Items that are past their due date | "Show me all overdue tasks" |
| Items created within the current week | "Everything created this week" |
| Items assigned to a specific person | "Tasks assigned to Natasha Romanoff" |
| Items with multiple conditions | "All critical items in the approval step due today" |

nodes.label: Adding advanced filters using AI
nodes.heading: Troubleshooting
nodes.snippet:
The generated filters are not what I expected.

- Why it happens — AI may have misinterpreted an unclear request.
- How to fix it — rephrase your request to be more direct and specific. For example, instead of _Show old tasks_, try _Show tasks created before October 1, 2025_. Including field names in your request often leads to more accurate results.

nodes.label: Adding advanced filters using AI
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:
- Creating and managing items
- Exporting items from a board
- Using board layouts

nodes.label: Importing items from a CSV file
nodes.heading: Importing items from a CSV file
nodes.snippet:
Importing items from a CSV file

CSV import lets you transfer items from a CSV file or a spreadsheet saved with a .csv extension into a Kissflow board. You can also use it to bring in data from other work management tools.

Once imported, each row in your CSV file becomes an item in your board, and each mapped column fills in a field on those items.

nodes.label: Importing items from a CSV file
nodes.heading: Prepare your CSV file
nodes.snippet:
Follow these guidelines when preparing a CSV file for import:

- Mandatory fields — if your board form has mandatory fields, you must map at least one CSV column to each of them.
- Field types — you can map CSV columns to these field types: Text, Text area, Number, Rating, Date, Date & time, Email, Currency, Yes/No, User, Multiple users, Single-select dropdown, Multi-select dropdown, Slider, and Checkbox.
- Delimiter — use only the comma (,) as the delimiter.
- Rows — the file can have up to 500 rows, excluding the header.
- Encoding — the file must have a .csv extension and use UTF-8 encoding.

nodes.label: Importing items from a CSV file
nodes.heading: Import a CSV file
nodes.snippet:
1. On the top-right section of the board, click the down arrow next to the New item button and select Import CSV.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

The New item button is named differently depending on the item type configured for the board.

2. Choose your .csv file from the file picker. Kissflow checks the file for corruption and delimiter errors — if an error is reported, fix it in the CSV file and upload it again.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

3. Click Next, then map the columns in your CSV file to the fields in your board. For example, map the email addresses in your CSV file to the Email field in your board.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

A CSV column can only be mapped to a board field of the same data type.

4. Choose how to handle duplicate items:
- Create a new copy of the item — adds the data as new items and keeps the original items in the board.
- Skip the item — skips rows that match existing items, based on a column you choose. Pick a column with distinct values (for example, Item ID or Phone number) so the system can tell whether an item already exists.

Email isn't available as a duplicate-matching column — choose a different distinct-value field.

- Replace the existing item — overwrites existing items that match, based on a column with distinct values that you choose.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

5. Click Finish. Your items begin importing — you can close the window and keep working while the import runs. You'll be notified by email when it completes.

media.alt: Dropdown next to the New item button showing the Import CSV option
Animation of choosing a CSV file and uploading it to the board
Column mapping step of the CSV import showing CSV columns matched to board fields
Duplicate handling step showing the options to create a copy, skip, or replace items

nodes.label: Importing items from a CSV file
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:
- Creating and managing items
- Exporting items from a board

nodes.label: Android devices
nodes.heading: Android devices
nodes.snippet:
Android devices

On Android, you can access your Kissflow account through your mobile browser, or install Kissflow as a Progressive Web App (PWA) for a more app-like experience.

nodes.label: Android devices
nodes.heading: Using a mobile browser
nodes.snippet: Open your preferred mobile browser and go to your account's URL: .kissflow.com.

nodes.label: Android devices
nodes.heading: Using a Progressive Web App (PWA)
nodes.snippet:
A Progressive Web App gives you a native app-like experience directly from your browser, without installing anything from the Play Store.

Recommended minimum: 2 GB of RAM on your device.

New features are added to the PWA on an ongoing basis. At any given time, it may support a smaller feature set than the full desktop experience.

Advantages of the PWA

- Faster updates — the PWA loads the latest version from the browser each time you use it, so you're not waiting on a staged rollout like you would with a native app update.
- Nothing to install — it runs in your mobile browser instead of installing as a separate app from the Play Store.

nodes.label: Android devices
nodes.heading: Installing the PWA

media.alt: Kissflow login page opened in a mobile browser
Browser menu icon

nodes.label: Android devices
nodes.heading: Enabling push notifications
nodes.snippet:
1. Tap your profile picture in the upper-right corner.
2. Tap the More options button .
3. Tap Notification settings.
4. Toggle on Push notifications.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: More options icon
Notification settings screen with the Push notifications toggle enabled

nodes.label: Android devices
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:

nodes.label: Form child tables in the PWA and native mobile app
nodes.heading: Form child tables in the PWA and native mobile app
nodes.snippet:
Form child tables in the PWA and native mobile app

Kissflow's PWA and native Android and iOS apps let you view and edit child tables inside process forms, board forms, and data forms. You can switch between two display modes depending on how you want to work with the data:

- Tabular view — the default view. Shows multiple rows at once as a table, making it easy to compare entries and edit several rows without expanding each one individually.
- Accordion view — keeps the child table compact by collapsing each row into its own expandable section, so you can focus on one row at a time.

media.alt: Form child table layouts in PWA

nodes.label: Form child tables in the PWA and native mobile app
nodes.heading: Switch between display modes
nodes.snippet:
1. Sign in to your Kissflow account using a PWA or native mobile app.
2. Open any form that contains a child table.
3. Tap the More options button at the top of the child table.
4. Tap Change display mode.
5. Select Tabular or Accordion.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: More options button at the top of a child table

nodes.label: Form child tables in the PWA and native mobile app
nodes.heading: Edit data in a child table
nodes.snippet:
1. Tap any cell in the child table to open the editing modal.
2. Update the field values as needed. Use the previous and next buttons in the modal to move between rows without closing it.
3. Changes are saved automatically as you edit.

nodes.label: Form child tables in the PWA and native mobile app
nodes.heading: View in fullscreen (native apps only)
nodes.snippet:
Fullscreen view is only available on the native Android and iOS apps. It isn't available on the PWA.

Fullscreen view rotates the child table into landscape mode so you can see more columns at once — useful on devices with limited screen space.

1. Sign in to your Kissflow account using a native mobile app.
2. Open any form that contains a child table.
3. Tap the More options button at the top of the child table.
4. Tap Enter fullscreen.
5. When you're done, tap the Exit fullscreen button to rotate the screen back to portrait mode.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Exit fullscreen button shown on a child table in landscape mode
Child table switching into fullscreen landscape view on a native mobile app

nodes.label: Form child tables in the PWA and native mobile app
nodes.heading: Reorder columns
nodes.snippet:
1. Sign in to your Kissflow account using a PWA or native mobile app.
2. Open any form that contains a child table.
3. Tap the More options button at the top of the child table.
4. Tap Reorder columns.
5. Press and hold the column header you want to move, then drag it to the desired position.
6. Release to drop the column in its new position, then tap Save.

Tap Reset at any point to restore the columns to their original order.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: More options button at the top of a child table

nodes.label: Form child tables in the PWA and native mobile app
nodes.heading: Delete rows in bulk
nodes.snippet:
You can select and delete multiple rows at once from the tabular view.

1. Sign in to your Kissflow account using a PWA or native mobile app.
2. Open any form that contains a child table.
3. In the tabular view, long-press any row to turn on row selection. A selection checkbox appears on the left of every row.
4. Tap the checkbox on each additional row you want to delete.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

5. Tap the Delete button at the top of the table.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

6. Confirm the deletion to remove the selected rows.

media.alt: Selection checkbox shown on the left of a row in the tabular view
Delete button at the top of a child table with rows selected

nodes.snippet:
iOS devices

On an iOS device, you can access your Kissflow account either through a mobile browser or by installing it as a Progressive Web App (PWA).

nodes.label: iOS devices
nodes.heading: Use a mobile browser
nodes.snippet: Open your preferred mobile browser and go to your account URL: .kissflow.com.

nodes.label: iOS devices
nodes.heading: Use a Progressive Web App (PWA)

media.alt: Kissflow Progressive Web App running on an iOS device

nodes.heading: Install the PWA on iOS
nodes.snippet:
1. Open Safari.
2. Type your account URL in the address bar — https://.kissflow.com.
3. Tap the Actions (share) icon.
4. Tap Add to Home Screen.
5. Tap Add.

iOS prevents Chrome from adding a PWA to the home screen. Use Safari to install the PWA.

nodes.label: Managing processes in the PWA
nodes.heading: Managing processes in the PWA
nodes.snippet:
Managing processes in the PWA

The dashboard in the Kissflow Progressive Web App (PWA) has two widgets for working with process items: My tasks and My items.

nodes.label: Managing processes in the PWA
nodes.heading: My tasks
nodes.snippet:
A task is an item that's awaiting your approval or some other action. The My tasks widget on the Home page shows your ongoing and overdue tasks, and you can add filters based on due date. Tap Show all in the My tasks section to open a detailed list of all your tasks.

Managing approvals

1. Tap the My tasks widget on the dashboard.
2. Tap a task from the list.
3. Tap Approve to approve the item.
4. Tap More actions to perform another action instead. From here you can reject, send back, or reassign the item.

Withdraw isn't available from More actions on a task. Withdrawing an item is only available to the person who initiated it, from the My items widget.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Other actions

1. Tap the My tasks widget on the dashboard.
2. Tap a task card from the list.
3. Tap the More options menu in the upper-right corner of the task.
4. Tap Progress to view the workflow and the task's current status.
5. Tap the Comments button to leave a comment about the task.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

If you don't have any tasks assigned yet, you can still stay updated by enabling push notifications.

media.alt: Task action sheet showing Approve and More actions options
Comments icon
Progress and Comments views opened from a task's More options menu

nodes.label: Managing processes in the PWA
nodes.heading: My items
nodes.snippet:
The My items widget shows the items you've initiated. You can filter them by status and creation timeline.

1. Tap the My items widget on the dashboard.
2. Tap an item card from the list.
3. Tap Withdraw or Reassign, based on what you want to do.
4. Tap the More options menu in the upper-right corner of the item to view its progress or duplicate it.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Withdraw, Reassign, and More options actions on an item opened from My items

nodes.label: Managing active sessions
nodes.heading: Managing active sessions
nodes.snippet:
Managing active sessions

The Active sessions page lists every device your account is currently signed in from, along with the browser, operating system, and IP address used for each session. Use it to spot sessions you don't recognize and sign them out.

nodes.label: Managing active sessions
nodes.heading: Access active sessions

media.alt: Active sessions page listing signed-in devices with their browser, OS, and IP details

nodes.label: Managing active sessions
nodes.heading: What each session shows
nodes.snippet:
Every entry in the list is a single device or browser where your account is signed in.

| Detail | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Device information | The web browser and, for computer sessions, the operating system version. |
| IP address | The IP address the session is connecting from. |
| First signed in on | The date and time the session first signed in. Hover over it to see the exact timestamp. |

Operating system version is only shown for sessions from a computer. For other device types, this field is left blank.

nodes.label: Managing active sessions
nodes.heading: Sign out of a session
nodes.snippet:
- Sign out of one session — click the More options button on that session and select Sign out. Signing out your current session prompts you to confirm before it signs you out.
- Sign out of every session — click Sign out from all active sessions to sign out of your current session and every other active session on all devices at once. This also asks for confirmation before it proceeds.

Review this page regularly and sign out any session you don't recognize. If you spot an unfamiliar session, also change your password.

nodes.label: Managing active sessions
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:
- Two-factor authentication
- Personal info, format settings, and task delegation
- API authentication

nodes.label: API Authentication
nodes.heading: API Authentication
nodes.snippet:
API Authentication

The API authentication page in your personal settings brings together everything you need to authenticate API calls to Kissflow — your account identifiers, your debug session key, and the API keys or access keys used to authorize requests.

nodes.label: API Authentication
nodes.heading: Access API authentication settings
nodes.snippet:
1. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner, then click My settings.
2. Click API authentication.

nodes.label: API Authentication
nodes.heading: Account identifiers
nodes.snippet:
Account identifiers help you uniquely identify your Kissflow account when making API calls or establishing sessions.

| Identifier | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Account ID | A unique alphanumeric key that identifies your Kissflow account. Copy it and use it to identify your account while making API calls. |
| Account domain | The domain address you use to access your Kissflow account directly in a web browser. |

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Account identifiers section showing the Account ID and Account domain fields with copy controls

nodes.label: API Authentication
nodes.heading: Debug session key
nodes.snippet:
If there's an issue with your account, the Kissflow team may need your debug session key to troubleshoot it.

1. Under Debug session key, click Show to reveal your unique key.
2. Click Copy and share the key with the Kissflow team to help them troubleshoot.

Share your debug session key only with the Kissflow team. Avoid sharing it anywhere else.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Debug session key section with the Show and Copy controls

nodes.label: API Authentication
nodes.heading: API keys
nodes.snippet:
API keys were Kissflow's original form of API authentication. Kissflow has since moved to the access key system for better security, so creating new API keys is no longer available.

Kissflow is phasing out API keys for all accounts on or after July 15, 2023. If you're still using API keys, create their equivalent access keys before then — existing API keys will continue to work only until they're deprecated.

Deleting an API key

1. Navigate to the API key you want to delete and click the More options button.
2. Click Delete to remove it permanently.

nodes.label: API Authentication
nodes.heading: Access keys
nodes.snippet:
An access key is a unique identifier used to authenticate a user, developer, or program calling the Kissflow API. Each access key consists of an access key ID and an access key secret — an ID-secret pair that grants programmatic access to Kissflow.

Anyone with your access key ID and secret can perform the same operations you can. Keep both values secure and never share them.

Creating an access key

Creating access keys requires the access key feature to be enabled for your account. Each user is limited to a maximum of 20 access keys.

1. Click your profile picture > My settings > API authentication > Access keys.
2. Click Create access key.
3. Enter a name for the access key.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

4. Set an expiry period for the key. Learn how to configure the maximum expiry period at the account level.
5. Copy the access key ID and secret and store them somewhere secure, or click Download credentials to save them as a file.
6. Click Done.
7. Review the confirmation message and click Proceed.

Your access key is now ready to use.

Viewing system-generated access keys

Some access keys are created automatically by Kissflow — for example, when you set up certain integrations. These system-generated keys are hidden by default.

1. Go to API authentication > Access keys.
2. Select the Show system-generated checkbox to view them.

System-generated keys are marked with a System flag beside their name and appear first in the list, ahead of the keys you've created yourself.

Managing an access key

1. Navigate to the access key you want to rename or delete and click the More options button.
2. Click Rename to change the key's name, or click Delete to remove it permanently.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Deleting an access key automatically revokes any connections established using that key.

media.alt: Access key creation dialog with a name field
More options menu on an access key showing the Rename and Delete actions

nodes.label: Changing the background theme
nodes.heading: Changing the background theme
nodes.snippet:
Changing the background theme

You can switch the background theme of your Kissflow account between System default, Light, Dark, and Dim modes from the Themes section.

nodes.label: Changing the background theme
nodes.heading: Change your theme
nodes.snippet:
1. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner of the page.
2. Select Themes.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

3. Choose a theme. Your selection is applied immediately.
4. Click Done to close the dialog.

The available themes are:

- System default — matches the theme set on your local device or browser.
- Light — a white background with dark text.
- Dark — a near-black background with light text.
- Dim — a dark grey background with light text.

The theme is applied as soon as you select it — Done only closes the dialog, so you can preview a theme before dismissing it.

The theme you select applies to all Kissflow accounts you access from the same browser.

media.alt: Profile menu open with the Themes option highlighted

nodes.label: Personal info, format settings, and task delegation
nodes.heading: Personal info, format settings, and task delegation
nodes.snippet:
Personal info, format settings, and task delegation

The General settings section of My settings is where you manage your own profile, password, display preferences, and task delegation — separate from account-wide settings, which admins manage.

nodes.label: Personal info, format settings, and task delegation
nodes.heading: Access your settings
nodes.snippet:
1. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner of the Home screen.
2. Click My settings.

The My settings page has three categories: General, Security, and Notification settings. General settings contains Personal info, Format settings, and Task Delegation.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: My settings page showing the General, Security, and Notification settings categories

nodes.label: Personal info, format settings, and task delegation
nodes.heading: Personal info
nodes.snippet:
The Personal info page lets you fill in your details, upload a profile picture, add a signature, and manage your email address.

Change your password

1. Click Change password.
2. Enter your current password and your new password.
3. Click Save.

Reset a forgotten password

1. Click Change password > Forgot password?
2. Click Send email.

You'll receive an email with instructions to reset your password.

The reset link in this email expires after 24 hours. If it expires before you use it, restart the Forgot password? flow to get a new one.

nodes.label: Personal info, format settings, and task delegation
nodes.heading: Format settings
nodes.snippet:
Format settings control how data and time-based values are displayed for your own account. They're separate from the account-wide format settings, which are typically managed by admins with account administration permissions (see Super Admin).

- Account time zone — used when a scheduled action in an integration runs, or when a deadline in a workflow is evaluated. Data from your processes, boards, and apps is displayed in this time zone.
- Language — the display language for your account. Kissflow supports more than 20 languages, including English, French, Spanish, German, Italian, Russian, Korean, Simplified Chinese, Japanese, Vietnamese, Thai, Polish, Portuguese, Slovak, Czech, Ukrainian, Hindi, and Romanian. Arabic is the exception — it's only available if your account has been enabled for it (see Languages supported by Kissflow).
- Date format — the display format for date fields across your account.

Number and currency format aren't configurable — they're always based on your browser's locale settings.

Click Save after choosing your preferences.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Format settings page with account time zone, language, and date format options

nodes.label: Personal info, format settings, and task delegation
nodes.heading: Task delegation
nodes.snippet:
Task delegation automatically reassigns your process tasks to another user while you're unavailable. Items already in your queue when you set up the delegation stay with you — only new items sent to you are redirected to the delegatee.

1. Click Add delegation.
2. Under Who do you want to delegate your tasks to?, select a user.
3. Specify how long you want your items delegated to that user.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Existing process items in your queue aren't delegated. Only new process items are redirected to the delegatee.

Edit or cancel a delegation

- Edit — click Edit delegation beside the delegated user's name and update the details.
- Cancel — click Cancel delegation. Kissflow resumes assigning new items to you as usual, but items already delegated remain with the delegated user.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

A Super Admin can override any delegation you set. Once a delegation has been overridden, a Super Admin can't override it a second time, but they can still edit their existing override.

media.alt: Task delegation setup showing the delegatee and duration fields
Delegation list showing Edit delegation and Cancel delegation actions

nodes.label: Personal info, format settings, and task delegation
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:
- Managing active sessions
- Two-factor authentication

- Format settings (Admin)

nodes.label: Two-factor authentication
nodes.heading: Two-factor authentication
nodes.snippet:
Two-factor authentication

Two-factor authentication (2FA) adds an extra layer of security to your Kissflow account by requiring more than just a password to sign in. Once 2FA is turned on for your account, you're prompted to enter a code along with your username and password.

nodes.label: Two-factor authentication
nodes.heading: 2FA enrollment
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow IAM Admins control 2FA enrollment for every user in the account, and can set it to one of three states:

| Enrollment | What it means |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Recommended | Users can choose whether to turn 2FA on for their own account. This is the default for new users. |
| Mandatory | 2FA is required to sign in. |
| Disable | 2FA is unavailable for the user. |

New users start with 2FA set to Recommended, not disabled — you can already turn 2FA on for your own account unless your IAM Admin has changed it.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Admin view of bulk 2FA enrollment options for users

nodes.label: Two-factor authentication
nodes.heading: Enabling 2FA for your account
nodes.snippet:
If your IAM Admin has made 2FA recommended or mandatory, you'll see the 2FA widget under My settings > Security. Turn on the widget, then choose how you want to receive your sign-in code:

- Email — the code is sent to your email address. Nothing else needs to be configured.
- Authentication app — the code is generated by an authenticator app on your mobile device or computer.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: My settings Security tab showing the 2FA widget with Email and Authentication app options

nodes.label: Two-factor authentication
nodes.heading: Setting up an authenticator app
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow supports standard authenticator apps such as Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password, and LastPass.

1. Turn on the 2FA widget under My settings > Security.
2. Next to Authentication app, click Set up.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

3. Open the authenticator app on your device and scan the QR code.
4. Enter the six-digit code generated by the app.
5. Click Verify to complete the setup.

To switch to a different authenticator app, click Change next to Authentication app and repeat the setup steps — scanning a new QR code and verifying it automatically replaces the previous app. You don't need to remove the old one first.

Deleting your authenticator app removes all its saved data. You'll need to set it up again from scratch if you want to use an authenticator app afterward — you can still sign in with an email code in the meantime.

media.alt: Authenticator app setup dialog with a QR code and a field for the six-digit code

nodes.label: Two-factor authentication
nodes.heading: Signing in with 2FA enabled
nodes.snippet:
Signing in with 2FA enabled is only slightly different from a normal sign-in. Enter your username and password as usual, then you'll see a second prompt depending on which 2FA mode you have enabled.

- Email code — enter the code sent to your email address. It's valid for five minutes.
- Authenticator app code — enter the six-digit code currently shown in your authenticator app.

nodes.label: Two-factor authentication
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:
- Managing active sessions
- Personal info, format settings, and task delegation

- Adding and managing users
- User roles and permissions

nodes.label: User notification settings
nodes.heading: User notification settings
nodes.snippet:
User notification settings

Notification settings lets you customize how and when you're notified about activity in your Kissflow account. You can control which channels you receive notifications on, and fine-tune notifications for individual processes, boards, datasets, apps, or portals.

nodes.label: User notification settings
nodes.heading: Global notifications
nodes.snippet:
1. Click your profile picture > My settings > Notification settings.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

2. Under Global notifications, turn the following toggles on or off:
- Email notifications — turn this ON to receive notifications via email.
- Web app notifications — turn this ON to receive notifications within the web application.
- Push notifications — turn this ON to receive push notifications. When you enable this for the first time, your browser may prompt you to allow desktop notifications from Kissflow — if you deny the prompt, push notifications won't work until you re-enable them in your browser settings.
- Bot notifications — turn this ON to receive notifications from the Kissflow bot.

Essential system notifications — such as those related to account security and user management — are prioritized and sent regardless of your channel settings, to make sure you don't miss critical updates.

The examples below illustrate the kind of system notifications that bypass your channel settings. The exact list may vary and isn't limited to these events.

| Notification | Trigger |
| --- | --- |
| User activated | A user's account is activated. |
| User deactivated | A user's account is deactivated. |
| Resend email verification | The verification email is resent. |
| Email address updated | A user's email address is updated. |
| User created | A new user is created. |
| Reset password | A user's password is reset. |
| 2FA status changed | A user's two-factor authentication status is changed. |
| Two factor verification | Two-factor authentication is enabled with an email code. |
| Domain verification | A verification email is sent to connect a domain. |
| Waiting for admin approval | A user creates a process, board, or dataset that requires admin approval. |
| Export | An export related to a user, board, dataform, dataset, or report is carried out. |

media.alt: My settings menu with Notification settings highlighted

nodes.label: User notification settings
nodes.heading: Notification channels

media.alt: Notification channels list with per-item Email, Web app, Push, and Bot toggles

nodes.label: User notification settings
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:
- Viewing notifications
- Personal info, format settings, and task delegation
- Admin notification settings
- Notification events in processes

nodes.label: Viewing notifications
nodes.heading: Viewing notifications
nodes.snippet:
Viewing notifications

Kissflow notifies you whenever there's activity on items assigned to you, mentions, and other updates across your account. You can review these from the notification bell in the top navigation bar, or open the full-screen notifications page to see everything in one place.

nodes.label: Viewing notifications
nodes.heading: View your notifications

media.alt: Bell icon
Top navigation bar with the notification bell icon highlighted
Notification pop-up showing the list of notifications with unread items highlighted and the unread count badge

nodes.label: Viewing notifications
nodes.heading: Manage your notifications
nodes.snippet:
- Mark a notification as read — Hover over a notification card and click the grey-filled circle that appears on its right side. The circle turns into an outline once the notification is read; click it again to mark it unread.
- Mark all as read — Click Mark all as read at the top of the pop-up. This link only appears when you have at least one unread notification, and it marks every notification currently loaded as read.
- Manage notification preferences — Click the Settings button to open your notification settings, where you can turn notifications on or off for each flow you're part of.
{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Settings icon

nodes.label: Viewing notifications
nodes.heading: View all notifications
nodes.snippet:
Click Show all at the bottom of the pop-up to open the full-screen notifications page.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Use the tabs at the top of the page to filter your notifications:

| Tab | Shows |
| --- | --- |
| All | Every notification you've received |
| Unread | Notifications you haven't marked as read |
| Mentions | Notifications where someone @mentioned you |

Click a notification card to go directly to its associated page, just like in the pop-up.

media.alt: Full-screen notifications page with tabs for All, Unread, and Mentions

nodes.label: Viewing notifications
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:
- User notification settings
- Homepage overview
- Watching a process item
- Personal info, format settings, and task delegation

nodes.label: Filtering items in a process
nodes.heading: Filtering items in a process
nodes.snippet:
Filtering items in a process

Instead of scrolling through every record, apply a filter to your process page to see only the items that match specific conditions. You can filter items inside the Draft, In progress, Completed, Withdrawn, and Rejected tabs.

nodes.label: Filtering items in a process
nodes.heading: Add a filter
nodes.snippet:
A filter can be a single condition, or a set of conditions grouped together. Only items that satisfy the filter are shown on the process page.

1. Click the Filter button on your process page.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

2. Click + Add a filter.
3. Select a field from the Select field dropdown, then define the condition:
- Choose an operator. Operators are contextually populated based on the field you select — for example Equal to, Not equal to, or Contains for a text field, and Is empty where applicable.
- Select a field or enter the value to check the condition against.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

4. To add more conditions to the same group, click Add new condition and choose whether the group uses AND or OR logic.

A filter group can use only one logical operator throughout. If a group has five conditions, they must all be joined with AND, or all with OR — you can't mix AND and OR within the same group.

5. Click + Add new filter to add as many filter groups as you need. Choose the logical operator that applies across the groups: AND requires every group to be true, and OR requires at least one group to be true.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

6. Click Apply to filter the items.

media.alt: Filter button on the process page toolbar
Operator dropdown populated based on the selected field's type
Multiple filter groups joined by an AND/OR operator

nodes.label: Filtering items in a process
nodes.heading: Filter by the current user
nodes.snippet:
When you're matching a text or user field against another field, use the Current User option to reference the person viewing the filter, instead of a fixed name.

While setting the value for a condition, choose Current User in the right-hand dropdown, then pick the attribute that fits your condition: Name, Email address, Manager, Status, or Job Title.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

For example, to check whether a department head is the person viewing the filter, set the condition to:

Department head — Equal to — Current User.Name

media.alt: Current User option with its attributes in the filter value dropdown

nodes.label: Filtering items in a process
nodes.heading: Filter by relative dates
nodes.snippet:
For date and time fields, you can filter against a relative value instead of a fixed date. While matching a date or time field against a value, click the More options button to see the relative options:

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

- Now
- Next 6 hours
- Next 12 hours
- Today
- Tomorrow
- Yesterday
- This week
- Last 7 days
- Next 7 days
- Last 14 days
- Last 30 days
- This month
- This quarter
- Next quarter
- This year

You can also open the calendar after choosing a relative value and adjust the date manually in the Value field.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: More options button that opens relative date values for a date/time field
Calendar picker for adjusting the Value field after choosing a relative date

nodes.label: Filtering items in a process
nodes.heading: Generate filters with AI
nodes.snippet:
Instead of manually building conditions, describe what you want to see in plain language and let AI build the filter for you.

1. Click the Filter button on your process page.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

2. In the filter pane, click Advanced filter.
3. In the text box under Generate advanced filter using AI, type your filtering criteria in plain language — for example, _My high priority assigned items_.
4. Click Generate. AI creates the corresponding filter conditions in the section below.
5. Once you're satisfied with the conditions, click Apply to see your filtered results.

AI-powered filter generation is available on the Enterprise plan. On the Basic plan, you can still build filters manually as described above.

Summarize and regenerate filters

Click Filter summary to get an AI-generated summary describing what your filter conditions represent. You can use this summary as a base for new prompts:

1. Click Edit summary.
2. Update the summary text to reflect your new requirement.
3. Click Regenerate to make AI create a new set of filter conditions.

For example, if the summary says _This filter shows items with priority High that are assigned to the current user_, you can edit it to say _This filter shows items with priority Critical that are assigned to the current user_ and regenerate the filter.

Example prompts

| To see... | You can type... |
| --- | --- |
| High-priority items assigned to you | "My high priority assigned items" |
| Items that are past their due date | "Show me all overdue tasks" |
| Items created within the current week | "Everything created this week" |
| Items assigned to a specific person | "Tasks assigned to Natasha Romanoff" |
| Items with multiple conditions | "All critical items in the approval step due today" |

Troubleshooting

Error: "Filter generation failed: Some required fields are hidden or couldn't be selected. Please make them visible in the Show/hide fields menu or refine your prompt."

- Why it happens — AI can only build filters using fields that are currently visible on your process. If your prompt mentions a field like "Name" or "Asset type" but that field is hidden in Show/hide fields, AI can't access it and the filter fails.
- How to fix it:
1. Close the filter pane.
2. Click the Show/hide fields button.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

3. In the dropdown, select the checkbox for every field you want to filter by.
4. Try generating the AI filter again.

The generated filters aren't what I expected.

- Why it happens — AI may have misinterpreted an unclear request.
- How to fix it — rephrase your request to be more direct and specific. For example, instead of _Show old tasks_, try _Show tasks created before October 1, 2025_. Including field names in your request often leads to more accurate results.

media.alt: Filter button that opens the filter pane
Show/hide fields button
AI-powered advanced filters - Overview video

nodes.label: Filtering items in a process
nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:
- Adding advanced filters using AI (Enterprise) — the same AI filter builder on boards
- Member actions in a process
- Watching a process item

nodes.label: Member actions in a process
nodes.heading: Member actions in a process
nodes.snippet:
Member actions in a process

As a process member, you initiate items and act on the steps assigned to you. This article covers where to find your items, the actions available on them, and how to perform bulk actions, duplicate items, and print a form.

nodes.label: Member actions in a process
nodes.heading: Accessing your items and tasks
nodes.snippet:
Every process has two primary tabs: Created by me and Assigned items.

Created by me

The Created by me tab shows all the items you've been active on. For each section, you can:

- Search items.
- Apply filters.
- Control which fields are shown or hidden from the Show/hide fields menu.
{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Table fields cannot be shown or hidden, and their associated data cannot be used for searching or filtering.

Click an item to open it and check its progress. You can also see the fields in the form that are made visible at your particular step.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Items are categorized in this order:

| Category | What it shows |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Drafts | Items you've started filling out but haven't submitted. You can edit values and delete, save, or submit the item. In table view, select the checkboxes before items to bulk delete them. |
| In progress | Items you've submitted that haven't finished the workflow. Opening one shows a progress bar in the upper-right of the form. Field values can't be changed. Depending on Process Admin settings, you may be able to reassign or withdraw the item. |
| Rejected | Items you created that were rejected. Clicking Restart after opening one shows the same data as a draft, which you can submit as a new item. |
| Withdrawn | Items you've withdrawn. You can restart these after opening them — restarting adds a note on the workflow that the item was restarted. |
| Completed | Items that have finished the workflow. |

Assigned items

The Assigned items tab shows all the steps assigned to you, categorized by step in the workflow. For each step assigned to you, you can search, apply filters, and control which fields are shown or hidden.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Depending on the permissions set by the Process Admin, you can edit or view the fields in the form. On the right side of the form is the status section — click Show all to see which steps are completed and which are still pending.

At the bottom of the form are the actions you can perform. These buttons are configured by the Process Admin, so their wording and visibility can differ by step:

- Approve/Done/Submit — advances the item to the next step. Forms may have validations that require you to adjust field values first.
- Reassign — lets you select someone else to complete this step.
- Reject — completely rejects the item. The person who started the item can restart it as a new draft.
- Send back — places the item at a particular step earlier in the workflow.
- Save — saves the values you've entered so you can come back to the item later.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Accessing participated items

To access all the items you've acted on, click More > Participated items.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Show/hide fields icon
Item opened from the Created by me tab showing its fields and progress
Assigned items tab listing steps assigned to the current user
Action buttons at the bottom of an assigned item's form
More menu open with Participated items option

nodes.label: Member actions in a process
nodes.heading: Initiate a new item
nodes.snippet:
1. Click + New item at the top right of the screen.
2. Fill in the form fields.
3. Click Submit.

You can also:

- Click Save to store your work in Drafts (accessible under Created by me) without submitting it.
- Click Discard to delete the item permanently from the system.

nodes.label: Member actions in a process
nodes.heading: Perform bulk actions
nodes.snippet:
You can approve, reassign, or reject multiple items at once from the Assigned items tab.

1. Go to the process and click the Assigned items tab.
2. Select the items you want to act on. You can also select the checkbox at the top, beside the column title, to select all items on the page.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Bulk actions apply only to the items you've selected. Check your process's grid page size if you need to act on more items than are currently loaded.

Approve items in bulk

Select the items you want to approve and click Approve. Bulk approval may take a few seconds; the final status is shown in a toast message at the top-right of the page.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Items that require your input — those with mandatory fields awaiting your input — are skipped during approval. Skipped items get a yellow exclamation mark once the bulk approval completes, so you can act on and reapprove them individually.

Reassign items in bulk

Select the items you want to reassign and click Reassign.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Reassigning an item to a specific user removes only your association with it — it doesn't affect the item's association with its other assignees, if any.

For example, if John and Linda are assigned to an item along with you, and you reassign it to Abraham, Abraham joins John and Linda as an assignee. You're the only one removed from the item.

Reassigning works differently when the user reassigning the item is a Process Admin. Learn more in admin actions in a process.

Reject items in bulk

From the Assigned items tab, select the items you want to reject, click Reject, and add relevant notes.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Multiple items selected in Assigned items with the bulk action bar visible
Toast message confirming bulk approval completion
Reassign action selected for multiple items
Reject action with a notes field for the selected items

nodes.label: Member actions in a process
nodes.heading: Delete an item

media.alt: Draft items selected with the Delete action available

nodes.label: Member actions in a process
nodes.heading: Duplicate an item
nodes.snippet:
You can duplicate a process item after initiating or submitting it. Duplicating creates a copy of the item with its values, subject to the scope of the initiator.

Click the Duplicate button at the top of the item page.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Only items in In progress or Completed states can be duplicated. The duplicate uses the last saved values for all fields.

Use case

Linda heads the HR team at an IT firm. It's appraisal season, and she's launching an Employee 1:1 Appraisal process. The form has generic fields like Employee name and Designation, plus sensitive fields like Current salary and Salary hike percentage.

She sets Current salary and Salary hike percentage to stay hidden until the item reaches the manager's approval step, so only the manager sees salary information when deciding on a hike. Once the process is published, Linda can duplicate an existing appraisal item instead of recreating one from scratch each time — without exposing sensitive fields during duplication.

media.alt: Duplicate icon
Duplicate button at the top of an open item

nodes.label: Member actions in a process
nodes.heading: Print a form
nodes.snippet:
1. Open an item and click the Print button at the top right.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

2. Select a paper size.
3. Select an orientation — Portrait or Landscape.
4. Set a preferred font size for the text.
5. Choose what to include in the printout:
- The item's status.
- The item's progress, with the option to exclude skipped steps.
- The item's comments.
- The item's associated tables.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

6. Under More settings, you can:
- Print every section of the form on a new page.
- Include the time zone.
- Print table rows as a table or as a comma-separated list.
- Select all or specific fields for printing.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

7. Click Print to get a paper printout or save it as a PDF.

media.alt: Print icon
Print button at the top right of an open item
Print options for status, progress, comments, and tables
More settings for time zone, row format, and field selection

nodes.heading: What's next
nodes.snippet:

nodes.label: Watching a process item
nodes.heading: Watching a process item
nodes.snippet:
Watching a process item

Watchers can stay informed about a process item without participating in its workflow. As an item initiator, if you need someone to have visibility into an item's progress but don't need them to approve, reject, or act on it, you can add them as a watcher.

For example, an employee submits a purchase request and wants their manager to stay informed about its progress and final outcome, without the manager taking part in the approval workflow. Adding the manager as a watcher lets them track the item and join the conversation without being assigned any workflow tasks.

Before anyone can add watchers to an item, a process admin must turn on Allow process members to watch items under the process's Security settings. Only users who are already part of the process — in any role — can be added as watchers.

nodes.label: Watching a process item
nodes.heading: Add a watcher
nodes.snippet:
Only the item's initiator can add watchers to it.

1. Open the process item.
2. Click the Watchers button on the item form.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

3. Search for and select a process user.
4. Click Add.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

An item can have a maximum of 10 watchers.

media.alt: Watchers icon on the item form header
Animation of adding a watcher to a process item

nodes.label: Watching a process item
nodes.heading: Remove a watcher
nodes.snippet:
The item's initiator can remove any watcher; a watcher can only remove themselves.

As the item initiator:

1. Open the process item.
2. Click the Watchers button on the item form.
3. Hover over the watcher's name.
4. Click Remove.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

If you're already a watcher, you can stop watching an item at any time. Open the item and click Stop watching. You're removed from the watcher list and no longer receive notifications for that item.

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

media.alt: Animation of removing a watcher from a process item
Animation of a watcher clicking Stop watching on a process item

nodes.label: Watching a process item
nodes.heading: What watchers can and can't do
nodes.snippet:
| Watchers can | Watchers cannot |
| --- | --- |
| View fields in read-only mode | Edit the form |
| Track workflow progress | Approve or reject items |
| View item history and status | Reassign tasks |
| View and add comments | Perform any workflow action |
| Mention participants and other watchers in comments | Mention external commenters in comments |

nodes.label: Watching a process item
nodes.heading: Notifications for watchers
nodes.snippet:
Watchers receive notifications when they're added or removed as a watcher.

Watchers may also be notified about other item events — such as being mentioned in a comment, someone reacting to their comment, or the item completing, being rejected, or withdrawn — depending on how the process's notification templates are configured. Check with your process admin if you're unsure which events trigger a notification for watchers in a specific process.

nodes.label: Watching a process item
nodes.heading: View your watched items

media.alt: Watchlist tab within a process
Watchlist tab in My items

nodes.label: Watching a process item
nodes.heading: Limitations
nodes.snippet:
- Watchers can't be added to draft items.

Whether restarted items retain their previous watchers, and whether removing a user from a process automatically removes them as a watcher on in-progress items, could not be confirmed against current product behavior. Verify these cases directly in your environment before relying on them.

nodes.label: Action required: Deprecation of Kissflow Employee Management Table
nodes.heading: Action required: Deprecation of Kissflow Employee Management Table
nodes.snippet:
Action required: Deprecation of Kissflow Employee Management Table

Effective mid-January 2024, Kissflow deprecated the Employee Management Table. After this date, employee details are no longer accessible through that table.

nodes.label: Action required: Deprecation of Kissflow Employee Management Table
nodes.heading: What changed
nodes.snippet:
All employee details previously available in the Employee Management Table are now accessible through the User Management table.

Access is more restricted than before: only User admins, IAM admins, and Super admins can view user details there. Previously, employee details could be shared with a wider set of users.

The _employee system field is no longer available for new use. Existing fields that referenced _employee were automatically replaced with user. Use user in new workflows going forward.

nodes.label: Action required: Deprecation of Kissflow Employee Management Table
nodes.heading: What to do
nodes.snippet:
If you were sharing employee details with other users through the Employee Management Table, let them know about this change and direct them to a User admin, IAM admin, or Super admin for anything they still need.

This change applies to accounts on the KF Digital Workflow, KF Process, KF Project, KF Workflow Lite, KF Workflow Suite, and KF Workflow plans.

If you have questions, contact Kissflow support.

nodes.label: Action required: End of support for Kissflow Chat connector in Integrations
nodes.heading: Action required: End of support for Kissflow Chat connector in Integrations
nodes.snippet:
Action required: End of support for Kissflow Chat connector in Integrations

Effective 20 December 2023, Kissflow Integrations no longer supports the Kissflow Chat connector. Any integration that depends on this connector stopped working after that date.

nodes.label: Action required: End of support for Kissflow Chat connector in Integrations
nodes.heading: What to do
nodes.snippet:
If you have integrations built on the Kissflow Chat connector, switch them to another supported chat platform. Kissflow Integrations currently supports connectors for Google Chat, Microsoft Teams, and Slack — reconfigure your integration to use one of these instead.

If you need help with the transition, contact Kissflow support.

nodes.label: Important Announcement: Deprecation of Self-Serve Option in Kissflow Subscription & Billing
nodes.heading: Important Announcement: Deprecation of Self-Serve Option in Kissflow Subscription & Billing
nodes.snippet:
Effective end of March, Kissflow discontinued the self-serve support option in the Subscription & billing section for the following plans:

- KFDW — Free, Basic, Advanced, Fully Loaded
- KF WORKFLOW — Basic, Advanced, Fully Loaded

nodes.label: Important Announcement: Deprecation of Self-Serve Option in Kissflow Subscription & Billing
nodes.heading: What changed
nodes.snippet:
The Manage plans button, previously used to purchase additional user licenses directly, has been removed. A Chat for help button now appears under Subscription details in the Subscription & billing section. Use it to reach support for additional licenses, plan changes, or billing-frequency adjustments.

Subscription details page after deprecation:

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

Card and billing details are no longer shown on the Subscription & billing page. Invoices can still be viewed and downloaded by super admins and billing admins.

If you have questions about this change, contact support through the Chat for help button.

nodes.label: Important reminder: Deprecation of Kissflow Chat and Spaces
nodes.heading: Important reminder: Deprecation of Kissflow Chat and Spaces
nodes.snippet:
Important reminder: Deprecation of Kissflow Chat and Spaces

Effective 1 July 2024, Kissflow Chat and Kissflow Spaces were discontinued.

nodes.label: Important reminder: Deprecation of Kissflow Chat and Spaces
nodes.heading: What changed
nodes.snippet: All functionality for both services was removed on this date, and access is no longer available.

nodes.label: Important reminder: Deprecation of Kissflow Chat and Spaces
nodes.heading: What to do
nodes.snippet: If you have questions about this change, contact Kissflow support.

nodes.label: Enhancing stability with static IP reservations
nodes.heading: Enhancing stability with static IP reservations
nodes.snippet: Kissflow sends outbound calls — including integration calls to external applications — through Google Cloud NAT. Before January 7, 2024, these calls used dynamic public IPs that could change over time, which meant customers who safelisted Kissflow IPs in their firewalls occasionally had to update their rules.

nodes.label: Enhancing stability with static IP reservations
nodes.heading: What changed
nodes.snippet: On January 7, 2024, Kissflow switched Cloud NAT to manual IP allocation, giving outbound traffic a fixed set of reserved static IPs. Firewall rules that safelist these IPs no longer need to be updated as Kissflow's underlying infrastructure changes.

nodes.label: Enhancing stability with static IP reservations
nodes.heading: Who was affected
nodes.snippet:
This change only affected customers who had safelisted Kissflow IPs in their firewall to allow Kissflow's integration calls into their network. Customers without that configuration were not affected.

Affected customers needed to safelist the new static IPs alongside the existing ones before the cutover, then remove the old IPs afterward. The current, authoritative list of IPs to safelist is maintained in Safelisting IPs for external data connections — use that page for your region's IPs rather than any list from this notice.

nodes.label: Enhancing stability with static IP reservations
nodes.heading: Change window
nodes.snippet:
The Cloud NAT migration was implemented on Sunday, January 7, 2024, during a period of minimal traffic. Integrations that were actively triggering during the cutover could have seen a disruption of less than one minute.

If you still see integration failures you believe are related to IP safelisting, contact Kissflow support.

nodes.label: Important information: Intermittent access issues due to security challenges
nodes.heading: Important information: Intermittent access issues due to security challenges
nodes.snippet: You may occasionally experience access issues in Kissflow due to network-level security checks on certain domains.

nodes.label: Important information: Intermittent access issues due to security challenges
nodes.heading: What happens
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow uses a network-level security layer to protect against malicious traffic and automated attacks. As part of this, incoming requests can be subjected to challenge mechanisms, such as browser validation or traffic inspection.

When a challenge fails, the request is blocked before it reaches Kissflow. This can cause the application to not load, or an action to fail, without a clear error message.

nodes.label: Important information: Intermittent access issues due to security challenges
nodes.heading: What this means for you
nodes.snippet:
- Pages may fail to load or appear unresponsive.
- Certain actions may not complete successfully.
- These issues can occur intermittently, depending on network conditions, IP reputation, or security policies.

nodes.label: Important information: Intermittent access issues due to security challenges
nodes.heading: What you can do
nodes.snippet:
Try these steps in order.

1. Refresh the page to retry the request.
2. Disable any VPN or proxy that may be affecting request routing.
3. Ask your IT team to review your network or firewall configuration.
4. If the issue persists, contact Kissflow support.

nodes.label: Important update regarding Kissflow's SSL certificate
nodes.heading: Important update regarding Kissflow's SSL certificate
nodes.snippet:
Sectigo, Kissflow's SSL certificate provider, is migrating its signing infrastructure to a new root certificate: Sectigo Public Server Authentication Root R46. This replaces the older trust chain built on AAA Certificate Services and USERTrust RSA Certification Authority.

Most users won't notice this change — modern browsers and operating systems already trust the new root. It only affects systems that can't receive regular OS or browser updates.

nodes.label: Important update regarding Kissflow's SSL certificate
nodes.heading: Who is affected
nodes.snippet:
You may be affected if you access Kissflow from:

- Legacy servers, embedded devices, or other isolated environments that don't receive regular OS/browser updates.
- Older browser or OS versions that predate native support for the new root (see the version table below).

Affected systems will start seeing SSL trust errors once the old chain is no longer supported:

In legacy environments or servers:
- SSL handshake failure
- SSL connection failed
- ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR

In older browsers:
- NET::ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID
- "Your connection is not private"

{/* TODO: verify screenshot matches current product UI */}

These errors happen because the affected system still relies on the old trust path — AAA Certificate Services → USERTrust RSA Certification Authority → Sectigo — which is being retired.

media.alt: SSL error shown in a browser

nodes.label: Important update regarding Kissflow's SSL certificate
nodes.heading: Deadlines
nodes.snippet:
After these dates, only the new Sectigo root (R46) will be trusted. Systems that haven't updated will lose access to Kissflow.

| Domain | Update by |
| --- | --- |
| kissflow.eu | February 13, 2026 |
| kissflow.com | February 27, 2026 |

Check the domain in your account's URL (the part before .kissflow.com or .kissflow.eu, for example yourcompany.kissflow.com) to see which deadline applies to you.

nodes.label: Important update regarding Kissflow's SSL certificate
nodes.heading: Check whether your environment is compliant
nodes.snippet:
Run this check from the specific browser or environment you use to access Kissflow.

Using OpenSSL:

__CODE_BLOCK_0

A compliant environment returns:

CODE_BLOCK_1__

If you don't see a return code of 0, import the new Sectigo R46 certificate into your system (see below).

Using a browser:

1. Open https://www.sectigo.com/.
2. Click the padlock icon → Certificate viewer → Details.
3. Check that the root certificate listed is Sectigo Public Server Authentication Root R46.

The following minimum versions natively support the new root:

| Browser | Minimum version | Release date |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Chrome | 120+ | Dec 2023 |
| Edge | 120+ | Dec 2023 |
| Firefox | 117+ | Aug 2023 |
| Safari (macOS/iOS) | macOS 14.4+ / iOS 17.4+ | Mar 2024 |
| Opera | 106+ | Dec 2023 |
| SRWare Iron | 120+ | Dec 2023 |

Even if your browser or OS meets the minimum version, we recommend running the verification check to confirm.

nodes.label: Important update regarding Kissflow's SSL certificate
nodes.heading: Import the new certificate
nodes.snippet:
If your system can't receive regular OS/browser updates, install the R46 root manually before your domain's deadline.

1. Download the Sectigo Public Server Authentication Root R46 certificate. Note the filename it saves as — you'll need it in the steps below.
2. Install it into your system's trusted root store:

- Windows — Open certmgr.msc → Trusted Root Certification Authorities → Import.
- macOS — Open Keychain Access → System Roots → Import.
- Linux — Copy the certificate to /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/ (converting it to PEM format first if it isn't already) and run:

``shell
sudo update-ca-certificates
`

- Java — Import the certificate into the Java keystore with keytool (this adds it alongside the existing trusted certificates — it doesn't replace the keystore file). Replace the -file value below with the actual filename you downloaded:

`shell
keytool -import -trustcacerts -keystore $JAVA_HOME/lib/security/cacerts \
-alias sectigoR46 -file SectigoPublicServerAuthenticationRootR46.crt
``

3. Restart the application or browser you use to access Kissflow.

If you connect to Kissflow via Docker or other containerized environments, update the certificate inside those images and containers as well.

If you need help with this update, contact Kissflow support.

nodes.label: Multi-language support in Kissflow
nodes.heading: Multi-language support in Kissflow
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow's interface is available in the following 20 languages.

| Simplified Chinese | 简体中文 |
| Italian | Italiano |
| Korean | 한국어 |

Every other language in the table is available to all accounts by default — no additional setup is required to select it.

nodes.label: Multi-language support in Kissflow
nodes.heading: Changing your interface language
nodes.snippet: Your personal language preference overrides your organization's default language, which admins set account-wide. See Personal info, format settings, and task delegation for how to change your own setting.

nodes.label: SLAs and support models
nodes.heading: How to reach support
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow Community is the primary channel for support. Access it in-product by clicking the (?) icon next to your profile picture, or go directly to community.kissflow.com. Use the Get Help section to post questions or report issues and defects — this is the main channel Kissflow support monitors, and questions about using the product get a prompt response.

Live chat is a separate, secondary channel for account-specific issues or defects. Open it from the same (?) icon in the top navigation bar — it launches a standalone chat widget rather than a thread inside Community. Chat availability can vary depending on your product and plan.

Only use email (support@kissflow.com) when you can't log in to Kissflow, for example during downtime. Emails are routed into the same support chat system and handled by support specialists.

nodes.label: SLAs and support models
nodes.heading: Status page
nodes.snippet: Kissflow's systems are actively monitored, and any issues are reported on the Kissflow status page. Check this page first if you're seeing service availability issues or downtime.

nodes.label: SLAs and support models
nodes.heading: Chat response times
nodes.snippet:
| Metric | Target |
| --- | --- |
| Conversations answered | Within 24 hours on business days |
| Average response time | About 30 minutes during business hours |

nodes.label: SLAs and support models
nodes.heading: Defect SLAs
nodes.snippet:
Once a product specialist confirms a defect, the following SLAs apply based on severity. All timelines exclude Saturdays and Sundays.

| Severity | Example | Ticket response | Workaround | Permanent fix |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Critical | The system is down | 4 hours | 4 hours (immediate workaround\*) | 48 hours |
| Highly important | An entire module is affected | 4 hours | 24 hours | 1 week |
| Medium | A feature within a module is affected | 8 hours | 3 business days | Next release |
| Low | Annoyances | 48 hours | As seen fit | As seen fit |

\* An immediate workaround is an acceptable solution to the specific problem reported.

Enterprise customers with large deals can have these SLAs written into a signed contract. Contact contact@kissflow.com for details.
